decks.label: Pure Physical vs Doom Knight (naive)
decks.notes: Average player's loop-10 brawler: a pile of reasonable STR/DEX attacks with NO sequencing plan. It builds rage with Reckless Strike (t2-attack-attack) and Bloodprice Strike (t2-attack-counter) but NEVER cashes a rage threshold (no Cleaver's Tax / Crimson Spiral), so the rage is wasted. Riposte (t1-counter, an aura that just deals 3-on-hit) and Shield Bash (t2-attack-defense, scales off armor it never builds) are off-fit filler that dilute the curve. No STR-ramp opener, no pierce against doom_knight's def 3 plan -- just flat hits in arbitrary order.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Doom Knight (optimized)
decks.notes: Master's tempo-ramp line, sequenced for FIXED ORDER. Cheap 1.2cd openers (Quickstep, Jab) sustain on the 50 stamina pool while the early Bloodlash Salvo (t3-attack-attack-counter, slot 3) banks +4 STR PER COMBAT -- multiplying every subsequent attack through C2's STR multiplier. Pinprick Volley + Triple Slash deliver pierce/high-OPS DEX volleys that ignore doom_knight's def 3. The win-condition is the explicit rage->pierce combo: Bloodprice Strike (t2-attack-counter) feeds rage so Cleaver's Tax (t3-attack-counter-counter, +20 STR Pierce when rage>=5) lands its bonus, with Mountain's Will and Quickstep Sigil's 30% haste closing the race. Same 11-card size as naive; quality is the only variable.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Iron Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Same loose brawler scaled up one boss. Against iron_golem (def 4 + periodic +4 shield) it leans on flat non-pierce hits that the armor ratchet chips away, and it picks Vein Splitter (t3-agility-attack-counter) -- a bleed-stacking card -- with NO bleed payoff, so its real value is just three small DEX hits. Generates rage (Reckless/Bloodprice) and again never spends it on a threshold. Riposte aura and Shield Bash remain off-fit. No STR-ramp front-load, no deliberate pierce to beat the shield.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Iron Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Built specifically to beat iron_golem's def4/shield with PIERCE. STR-ramp opener (Bloodlash Salvo slot 3, +4 STR/combat) front-loaded so the multiplier compounds. The win-condition stack is all pierce/threshold: Cleaver's Tax (+20 Pierce at rage>=5, fed by Bloodprice Strike), Granitewrath (t3-attack-counter-earth: 14 STR + Vengeance 10 Pierce), Mountain's Will (20 STR + 8 Pierce when armored), and Pinprick Volley (5 DEX Pierce x3). Pierce skips the shield ratchet that the naive deck loses tempo to. Quickstep Sigil's 30% haste accelerates the whole fixed-order engine. 12 cards, matched to naive.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Lizard King (naive)
decks.notes: Loop-30 brawler grab-bag. Good raw STR cards but no engine: it never front-loads Bloodlash Salvo's STR ramp, never converts its rage (Reckless + Bloodprice) into a threshold payoff, and still carries Vein Splitter (bleed, unsupported) and Shield Bash (armor-scaling, no armor) as dead-weight. Against lizard_king (multi-hit + enrage at 30% HP) the slow uncoordinated damage lets the boss reach enrage before the deck closes. Plain, unsequenced order.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Lizard King (optimized)
decks.notes: Race-to-burst build to kill lizard_king BEFORE its 30%-HP enrage. Bloodlash Salvo (slot 3) banks +4 STR/combat early; every later card rides that multiplier. Dense pierce/high-damage finisher core -- Cleaver's Tax (rage>=5 -> +20 Pierce), Mountain's Will (20 STR + 8 Pierce), Granitewrath (14 + 10 Pierce Vengeance), Slag Maul (16 STR) -- front-loads damage so the boss dies fast and never enrages. Bloodprice Strike sets up the rage threshold; Quickstep Sigil's 30% haste compresses the kill window. Slag Maul's burn is incidental, the card is here for its raw 16 STR. 13 cards, matched to naive.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Desert Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Loop-50 deck against desert_golem (450 HP, def4, big +10 shield every 7s). It does finally include Bloodlash Salvo but BURIES it at slot 12 -- in FIXED ORDER the STR ramp almost never fires before the fight is decided, so the multiplier is wasted. Mostly non-pierce flat hits that the heavy shield absorbs, rage still never cashed to a threshold, plus Vein Splitter/Riposte/Shield Bash as off-archetype filler. Reasonable cards, no plan -- the classic naive failure mode against a high-HP shielded boss.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Desert Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Pierce-heavy ramp tuned for desert_golem's 450 HP + 10-point shield wall. STR engine is front-loaded: Bloodlash Salvo at slot 3 starts the +4 STR/combat snowball immediately (the exact mistake the naive deck makes by burying it). Win-condition is maximum pierce-through-armor: Cleaver's Tax (+20 Pierce at rage>=5), Earthcleaver (t3-attack-defense-earth: 18 STR Pierce, +8 more when armored), Granitewrath (14 + 10 Pierce), Mountain's Will (20 STR + 8 Pierce). Bloodprice Strike supplies the rage gate; double Quickstep + Quickstep Sigil 30% haste keep stamina-cheap attacks flowing to out-DPS the shield regen. 14 cards, matched to naive.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Ancient Iron Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Endgame loop-70 pile against boss_iron_golem (470 HP, def4, +12 shield every 7s, enrage at 35%). Lots of T3 attacks but assembled with zero synergy: Bloodlash Salvo again sits deep (slot 12) so its STR ramp barely matters, rage from Reckless/Bloodprice is never converted at a threshold, and Vein Splitter is an unsupported bleed card. Riposte aura and Shield Bash are still dead fillers. Against the heaviest shield in the rotation the non-pierce flat damage stalls -- a likely timeout, the canonical naive endgame wall.

decks.label: Pure Physical vs Ancient Iron Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Maxed pure-physical pierce engine for the final golem's 470 HP + 12 shield + 35% enrage. Two-part win condition. (1) Ramp: Bloodlash Salvo front-loaded at slot 3 stacks +4 STR/combat early, and it can recur (non-exhaust) so STR climbs hard, multiplying everything via C2. (2) Pierce wall-breakers in fixed order so the +12 shield is bypassed every cycle: Cleaver's Tax (+20 Pierce at rage>=5, gated by Bloodprice Strike's rage), Earthcleaver (18 +8 Pierce), Granitewrath (14 +10 Pierce), Mountain's Will (20 +8 Pierce), Slag Maul (16 STR raw). Pinprick Volley + Triple Slash + Quickstep Sigil's 30% haste keep cheap DEX pierce flowing to push past the enrage threshold quickly. 15-card forge-cap deck, matched in size to the naive build so mastery is the only variable.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Doom Knight (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: an average player throws together on-theme bleed and rage cards but never closes the loop. It applies bleed (Sidestep & Slash, Razor Stance aura, Berserker's Ledger self-bleed) and accumulates rage (Reckless Strike 3, Bloodprice 2, Crimson Tithe 3) but carries NO rage-threshold payoff card (no Crimson Spiral / Cleaver's Tax), so the accumulated Fury only adds its small flat per-stack hero damage and is otherwise wasted. There is also NO bleed multiplier (no Vein Splitter) to snowball stacks toward the 6-cap. Crimson Tithe (peakOPS 3.75) and Kindle Strike (a burn card with no burn support) are the slightly-off filler an average player includes 'because it says rage/bleed'. Order is unsequenced: the aura (Razor Stance) sits mid-deck instead of opening, and the biggest hitter (Berserker's Ledger) lands before any bleed is on the target, so its self-bleed feeds nothing. Doom Knight's def 3 chips every small physical hit.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Doom Knight (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: deliberate sequencing builds the Fury gate, then snowballs bleed, then cashes both. Opener Reckless Strike + Bloodprice Strike front-loads rage (3 + 2/str) AND refunds stamina (Bloodprice +1 stam) so the curve sustains on the 50-stam pool; by the time Crimson Spiral (5+ rage gate) and Cleaver's Tax (5+ rage gate) fire, Fury is already past threshold so they deliver their full 24 / 32 Pierce — Pierce ignores Doom Knight's def 3 and the 50% enrage window. Sidestep & Slash seeds early bleed so Vein Splitter (each hit applies +1 extra bleed when the enemy already bleeds — peakOPS 134) compounds stacks toward the 6-cap, and Razor Cadence's 3 hits each apply bleed with Vengeance bonuses on Doom Knight's swings. Razor Stance aura keeps reapplying bleed on every auto-hit. Every card is on-archetype and ordered builder -> bleed-engine -> threshold finisher; cheap 1-cost low-CD cards lead so nothing is skipped for lack of stamina.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Iron Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive vs Iron Golem (def 4 + a +4 armor shield every 8s — an armor RATCHET). The average player leans on raw physical strikes (Jab, Reckless Strike, Berserker's Ledger, Quickstrike) plus loose bleed/rage, but almost none of it pierces: every non-Pierce hit eats the golem's def 4 AND fights the growing shield, so chip damage stalls. It still has NO rage payoff (no Crimson Spiral/Cleaver's Tax) so rage is wasted, and NO bleed multiplier, so bleed never reaches the cap where its quadratic DoT (which bypasses armor) would matter. Crimson Tithe, Kindle Strike, and Riposte (a 6-second aura that does almost nothing here) are off-theme filler. This deck is the 'can the average loop-20 player beat the second boss' realism check and is expected to stall against the shield ratchet.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Iron Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized vs the Iron Golem armor ratchet. The win condition is bleed DoT (which ticks past armor entirely) plus Pierce finishers — the exact answer to a +4-armor-per-8s shield. Reckless Strike + Bloodprice open the Fury gate fast; Sidestep & Slash + Vein Splitter + Razor Cadence + Razor Stance pile bleed to the 6-cap so the quadratic ~0.75n(n+1) DoT does most of the work without touching armor; Crimson Spiral (24 Pierce) and Cleaver's Tax (12 + 20 Pierce once 5+ rage) punch through the def 4 and shield outright. Bloodtide Mend adds bleed plus light sustain to survive the 10-dmg swings while the DoT cooks. Every Vengeance trigger fires on the golem's attacks (bleed swing x2 + extra applications). Same 12-card band as the naive deck, so build quality — Pierce + bleed-multiplier + rage gate — is the only variable.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Lizard King (naive)
decks.notes: Naive vs Lizard King (370 HP, def 3, attacks twice at 0.6x each, enrages at 30%). The double-hit means Vengeance and bleed-swing triggers are constantly available — but the average player doesn't exploit them: this deck has the bleed appliers (Sidestep & Slash, Razor Stance, Berserker's Ledger) and rage feeders (Reckless Strike, Bloodprice, Crimson Tithe) yet NO rage-threshold payoff and NO Vengeance-scaling finisher, so all that triggered value evaporates. Shield Bash (deals damage = your armor, but this deck barely makes armor) and Kindle Strike are off-curve picks. Rage caps out unused; bleed never multiplied. Picks are reasonable-looking but the combo is never assembled.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Lizard King (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized vs Lizard King — built to abuse the boss's double-attack. Every enemy swing is a Vengeance trigger, so Razor Cadence (Vengeance: +5 dmg, +1 bleed), Sidestep & Slash (Vengeance: +3), and the constant just-attacked bleed-swing x2 all fire on near-every tick — the boss feeds our engine. Reckless Strike + Bloodprice open the Fury gate; Vein Splitter snowballs bleed to the cap; Crimson Spiral (24 Pierce at 5+ rage) and Cleaver's Tax (32 Pierce at 5+ rage) blow past def 3 and the 30% enrage. Crimson Cascade adds a bleed kill-spread / extra application and Bloodtide Mend gives bleed + light sustain through the multi-hit pressure. Razor Stance reapplies bleed on every auto. Builder -> snowball -> Vengeance-fed finisher ordering; cheap leads keep the fixed-order curve affordable.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Desert Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive vs Desert Golem (450 HP, def 4, +10 armor shield every 7s — a HARSH ratchet). The average late-game player still swings raw physical (Jab, Reckless Strike, Berserker's Ledger, Quickstrike) into a wall of armor: with a +10/7s shield, almost every non-Pierce hit is fully absorbed, and this deck has essentially no Pierce and no detonator. It also still lacks a rage payoff and a bleed multiplier, so the one armor-independent damage source (bleed DoT) never reaches the cap where it could carry. Two copies of Quickstep, Crimson Tithe-tier filler, and Shield Bash (no armor base) are off-curve. Expected to time out / stall — the diagnostic case for 'late physical decks vs golem shields'.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Desert Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized vs Desert Golem's +10-armor ratchet — the bleed/rage answer to heavy armor. Bleed DoT bypasses armor completely, so Sidestep & Slash + Vein Splitter (+1 bleed per hit when target already bleeds) + Razor Cadence + Razor Stance race the bleed pool to the 6-cap and let the quadratic DoT chew the 450 HP independent of the shield. The Fury gate (Reckless Strike + Bloodprice opener) unlocks Crimson Spiral (24 Pierce) and Cleaver's Tax (12 + 20 Pierce), both Pierce so they ignore def 4 + shield. Tremor Detonate is the key tech vs this boss: it consumes the entire bleed pool and deals 3(str) Pierce per stack as one armor-ignoring burst, converting the capped bleed into immediate Pierce damage before the shield re-applies. Crimson Cascade + Bloodtide Mend add bleed and sustain through the 10-dmg swings. Same 14-card band as naive; quality (Pierce + detonator + bleed multiplier + rage gate) is the variable.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Ancient Iron Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive vs Ancient Iron Golem (470 HP, def 4, +12 armor shield every 7s, enrages at 35%) — the toughest armor wall in the game. The average player's physical pile (Jab, Reckless Strike, Berserker's Ledger, Quickstrike, Bloodlash Salvo) is almost entirely absorbed by the def-4 + +12/7s shield; with no Pierce, no detonator, no rage payoff and no bleed multiplier, the only armor-independent damage (bleed) never snowballs to the cap. Bloodlash Salvo ramps str but str doesn't beat a shield ratchet, and Shield Bash / Crimson Tithe are dead. This is the worst-case realism check: a normal endgame bleed deck should clearly LOSE here, isolating how much the optimized build's Pierce+detonator package matters.

decks.label: Bleed/Rage vs Ancient Iron Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized vs the Ancient Iron Golem — the full bleed/rage endgame package built specifically to ignore a +12-armor ratchet. Two armor-independent win conditions stack: (1) bleed DoT to the 6-cap via Sidestep & Slash + Vein Splitter (bleed multiplier) + Razor Cadence + Razor Stance, whose quadratic tick never touches the shield; (2) Pierce finishers gated on Fury — Crimson Spiral (24 Pierce) and Cleaver's Tax (12 + 20 Pierce) — unlocked by the Reckless Strike + Bloodprice opener and reinforced by Wrathshell Vow's ramping rage so the 5+ gate stays open all combat. Tremor Detonate cashes the entire bleed pool into one big Pierce burst (3 str per stack) to cut through the 35% enrage. Crimson Cascade + Bloodtide Mend supply extra bleed and the sustain to outlast 12-dmg swings while the DoT does the lifting. Same 15-card cap band as naive; the only difference is mastery: Pierce + detonator + bleed-multiplier + sustained Fury gate vs raw absorbed physical.

decks.label: Stonewall Apprentice (boss1, naive)
decks.notes: Naive armor deck: it stacks lots of [armor] (Guard, Bulwark Vow, Parrying Stance, Tremor Dash, Iron Reckoning, Thornwall) but has NO real armor->damage payoff. The only conversion is one mid Shield Bash (deal = armor) buried in slot 7 with cd1.6, and it never reaches a true finisher. Most armor just sits unused as wasted survivability (C3: armor is race-losing on its own). Damage comes from off-theme generic attacks (Jab, Quickstep, Reckless Strike) that ignore the armor the deck is built around. No threshold sequencing, no +50% armor aura, no Mountain's Answer/Aegis. This is what a normal player throws together: 'I have a wall, I'll just keep hitting' — and it stalls or times out on the boss because armor produces no offense.

decks.label: Mountain's Answer Engine (boss1, optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized armor->damage combo: front-loaded cheap 1s low-cd builders (Guard cd1.6, Parrying Stance cd1.3 +stam, Tremor Dash) ramp [armor] fast and sustainable on the 50-stamina pool. Granite Lunge and Bramble Bulwark are dual-purpose (gain armor AND convert it: Lunge +1 dmg/4 armor, Bramble 6 Pierce at armor>=10). The win condition is the ONE allowed armor->damage finisher line: Aegis of Returning Wrath (22 armor + Pierce that scales per 4 armor) and Mountain's Answer (gain 26 armor -> if armor>=32 deal 22 Pierce). By the time those resolve, the earlier stackers have pushed armor over the 32 threshold so the finisher actually FIRES. Shield Bash (deal = armor) and Body Slam Vow (+1 dmg per armor) are extra converters that turn the wall into burst. Same 10-card size as the naive deck, so build quality — threshold-aware sequencing + a real finisher — is the only variable.

decks.label: Iron Turtle (boss2, naive)
decks.notes: Naive: piles on pure-defense armor cards (Guard, Bulwark Vow, Parrying Stance, Iron Reckoning, Cyclone Ward, Veil of Steps, Phalanx Drift) for a huge wall, but the offense is generic and armor-blind. The lone converter, Shield Bash, sits in slot 12 and almost never gets drawn in fixed order. Against iron_golem's defense-affinity armor ratchet, plain Jab/Quickstep/Reckless Strike chip far too slowly and the fight times out — classic 'race-losing armor with no finisher' (C3). No +50% aura, no Mountain's Answer, no threshold gating; the player just trusts the wall to win, and it can't close.

decks.label: Reforged Avalanche (boss2, optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: the synergy core is Reforge Vow in slot 4 — its +50% armor-gain aura is placed BEFORE the big stackers (Bedrock Bulwark 16+Brace6, Aegis 22, Mountain's Answer 26) so every later gain is amplified, blasting past Mountain's Answer's armor>=32 threshold and Bramble's >=10 gate. Cheap 1s builders (Guard, Parrying Stance +stam, Tremor Dash, Granite Lunge) front-load the ramp and sustain stamina. The whole deck converts the wall: Granite Lunge / Bramble Bulwark / Body Slam Vow / Shield Bash all read armor, and Aegis + Mountain's Answer are the C3 armor->damage finisher payoff. Identical 12-card size to the naive turtle; the difference is the aura-before-stack sequencing and a finisher that actually detonates the wall, which lets it out-damage iron_golem's ratchet instead of timing out.

decks.label: Bulwark Brawler (boss3, naive)
decks.notes: Naive late-game wall: lots of armor (Guard, Bulwark Vow, Parrying Stance, Iron Reckoning, Veil of Steps, Cyclone Ward, Phalanx Drift, Last Stand Bulwark) but the armor never becomes damage. Last Stand Bulwark only converts when below 50% HP and is parked in slot 13. Offense is off-theme generic attacks. No +50% aura, no Mountain's Answer/Aegis, no threshold awareness. Against lizard_king this stalls: the deck survives but can't generate enough offense from its armor, so it loses the damage race / times out (C3 in action — armor with no finisher is a stall plan).

decks.label: Tectonic Wall-Breaker (boss3, optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: Reforge Vow's +50% armor aura (slot 4) front-runs Bedrock Bulwark, Aegis and Mountain's Answer so the stack rockets past every threshold (Mountain's Answer >=32, Mountain's Will >=20, Bramble >=10). Cheap 1s builders open and sustain. The deck is a true armor->damage machine: Granite Lunge, Bramble Bulwark, Body Slam Vow, Shield Bash, Mountain's Will (deal 20, +8 Pierce at armor>=20) and the two finishers (Aegis Pierce-per-4-armor, Mountain's Answer 22 AoE Pierce) all read the wall and turn it into burst. Same 13-card band as the naive Bulwark Brawler; the only difference is synergy: aura-before-stack sequencing plus a finisher suite that converts armor into a kill instead of letting it sit.

decks.label: Desert Fortress (boss5, naive)
decks.notes: Naive deep-run wall: maximal armor accumulation (Guard, Bulwark Vow, Parrying Stance, Iron Reckoning, Veil of Steps, Cyclone Ward, Phalanx Drift, Galeward, Thornwall, Last Stand Bulwark) but ZERO dedicated finisher — no Mountain's Answer, no Aegis, no +50% aura. The only armor readers are conditional/late (Last Stand at <50% HP, slot 13; Shield Bash slot 14). Damage comes from a thin generic-attack package that can't out-pace desert_golem's HP. The wall holds for a long time then loses the race — the textbook C3 failure mode of armor without a conversion win condition.

decks.label: Avalanche Engine (boss5, optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized deep build: Reforge Vow aura (+50% all armor gain, slot 4) sits ahead of every big stacker so Bedrock Bulwark, Aegis and Mountain's Answer overshoot their thresholds with margin (crucial at boss5 scaling). A wide conversion suite reads the wall at multiple gates: Granite Lunge (per 4), Bramble (>=10), Mountain's Will (>=20), Cliffwind Maul (deal 12, +10 Pierce at armor>=12), Body Slam Vow (per armor), Shield Bash (= armor), plus the two true finishers Aegis (Pierce per 4 armor) and Mountain's Answer (22 AoE Pierce at armor>=32). Cheap 1s openers keep stamina afloat across the long fight. Same 14-card band as the naive Desert Fortress — quality (aura-led sequencing + a real finisher chain) is the isolated variable, and it converts the survivable wall into actual kill pressure.

decks.label: Endgame Turtle (boss7, naive)
decks.notes: Naive endgame wall: the most armor of any deck (Guard, Bulwark Vow, Parrying Stance, Iron Reckoning, Veil of Steps, Phalanx Drift, Cyclone Ward, Galeward, Stormgate, Thornwall, Last Stand Bulwark) yet it cannot convert it. No +50% aura, no Mountain's Answer, no Aegis — the win condition is missing entirely. Offense is a token generic-attack package (Jab, Reckless Strike, Quickstep) plus the conditional Last Stand and a lone slot-15 Shield Bash. Against boss_iron_golem's heavy HP and defense-affinity ratchet this is a guaranteed stall/timeout: armor without a finisher is pure survival with no kill (C3).

decks.label: Mountain's Verdict (boss7, optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized endgame: Reforge Vow's +50% aura (slot 4) leads, so the heavy stackers (Bedrock Bulwark 16, Aegis 22, Mountain's Answer 26) all inflate well past the armor>=32 finisher gate even under boss7 scaling. The conversion chain is deep and multi-gated — Granite Lunge (per 4), Bramble (>=10), Mountain's Will (deal 20 +8 at >=20), Cliffwind Maul (+10 Pierce at >=12), Body Slam Vow (per armor), Shield Bash (= armor) — and ends on the C3 finisher line: Aegis (Pierce per 4 armor) and Mountain's Answer (22 AoE Pierce). The capstone is Citadel Inferno (consume ALL armor -> 2 Pierce per armor AoE), a one-shot detonation of a 60+ wall that closes a fight the naive turtle would time out. Same 15-card cap as the naive Endgame Turtle, so the only variable is mastery: aura-first sequencing plus an actual armor->damage win condition versus a wall that never converts.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Doom Knight (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: throws together physical hitters with a burn applier (t2-attack-fire Kindle Strike) and a self-bleed/rage card (t2-attack-attack Reckless Strike) but NEVER cashes them in — there is no burn detonator and no rage-threshold finisher, so the burn/bleed only tick for chip while doom_knight's counter affinity punishes the long fight. Razor Stance (t2-counter-counter) is a bleed aura buried mid-deck with no payoff that scales off the bleed it applies. t1-water (Mend) is an off-theme heal a normal player adds 'for safety' that wastes a slot. Card order is craft-order, not a curve.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Doom Knight (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: front-loads cheap 1s physical openers (Reckless Strike, Jab, Quickstrike) to build rage and pressure immediately, then the burn line is fully assembled — Flame Dart + Cinder Thrust APPLY burn, Pyre Surge (t3-air-fire-fire) DOUBLES and amplifies it (+2/tick), and Cinderlance (t3-attack-fire-fire) DETONATES the whole pool with 8 str x2 pierce on top, beating doom_knight's def 3. The bleed/rage half is real: Vein Splitter (t3-agility-attack-counter) snowballs bleed while Reckless/Bloodprice bank rage, then Crimson Spiral (t3-counter-counter-counter) fires its rage>=5 threshold for 24 str pierce + 4 bleed as the finisher before the 50% enrage matters. Same 11-card size as the naive build, so quality is the only variable.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Iron Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: more burn appliers (Kindle Strike, Cinder Thrust, Wickfencer) but STILL no detonator and no Pyre Surge, so against iron_golem's def 4 + shield ratchet (+4 armor/8s) the non-pierce physical hits get eaten and the burn only trickles. Bleed aura (Razor Stance) and self-bleed (Reckless Strike) have no threshold payoff. t1-water heal and t2-defense-defense are reactive slot-fillers a normal player adds when losing, not a plan. Ordering is unsequenced.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Iron Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: same two-engine plan tuned for iron_golem's armor ratchet — the win condition is PIERCE. Burn is applied early (Flame Dart, Cinder Thrust), amplified by Pyre Surge, then DOUBLE-detonated: Cinderlance (8 str x2 pierce + 3 pierce/burn) mid-fight and Supernova (t3-fire-fire-fire, 5 int pierce per burn, exhaust) as a banked nuke that ignores the +4 shield entirely. Rage from Reckless/Bloodprice feeds Crimson Spiral's pierce finisher (24 str pierce). Vein Splitter keeps a bleed clock running so the shield interval cannot reset the race. Same 12-card band as naive.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Lizard King (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: a pile of physical-with-burn-rider cards (Cinder Thrust, Wickfencer, Slag Maul) and a couple bleed sources, but the player never built the combo: no Pyre Surge amplify, no burn detonator, no rage finisher. Against lizard_king's 2-hit + 30% enrage the slow chip damage lets the boss out-tempo. t1-water and t2-defense-defense are panic slots. Each card is individually fine but they do not multiply each other.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Lizard King (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: burst-races the enrage. Cheap physical opener banks rage fast, the burn engine (Flame Dart + Cinder Thrust appliers -> Pyre Surge double/amplify -> Cinderlance + Supernova detonators) delivers two big pierce spikes, and the bleed engine doubles up (Vein Splitter + Razor Cadence both snowball bleed and self-buff dex) feeding Crimson Spiral's rage>=5 finisher. The plan front-loads damage to kill before the 30% enrage instead of trading turns. Same 12-card band as naive.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Desert Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: deep deck, lots of physical+burn riders, but the structural flaw is fatal at boss5 — desert_golem stacks +10 armor every 7s and this deck has ZERO pierce and ZERO detonator, so raw 14-16 damage hits get shredded by the constantly-refreshing armor and the un-amplified burn never out-paces the shield cycle. Self-bleed and bleed-aura cards have no finisher. t1-water + t2-defense-defense + Forge Strike (t3-attack-defense-fire) are 'survive longer' slots that just prolong a losing race.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Desert Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: built specifically to defeat the +10 armor ratchet with PIERCE finishers. The whole burn pool is amplified by Pyre Surge then dumped as pierce by Cinderlance (pierce per burn) and Supernova (5 int pierce per burn) — armor is irrelevant to those. Crimson Spiral delivers 24 str PIERCE on the rage threshold, and Mountain's Will (t3-attack-earth-earth) closes with 20 str + 8 pierce. Bleed from Vein Splitter/Razor Cadence is a separate armor-ignoring DoT clock. Rage banks from Reckless Strike + Bloodprice keep Crimson Spiral online. Same 13-card band as naive; the difference is every payoff bypasses armor.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Ancient Iron Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: the endgame version of the same mistake. Big physical+burn riders (Cinder Thrust, Wickfencer, Slag Maul, Bloodlash Salvo, Berserker's Ledger) but no pierce and no detonator, so boss_iron_golem's +12 armor/7s shield plus def 4 plus 35% enrage hard-walls it — non-pierce hits land for almost nothing once the shield stacks. Burn is applied but never doubled or detonated; bleed/rage are applied but never cashed at a threshold. The two defensive/heal slots (t1-water, t2-defense-defense) only delay the inevitable. Unsequenced order.

decks.label: Mixed Bruiser vs Ancient Iron Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: maximum pierce + maximum DoT to break the heaviest armor ratchet in the game (+12/7s, def 4, 35% enrage). Burn appliers feed Pyre Surge (double + amplify) and TWO pierce detonators (Cinderlance, Supernova) that ignore the shield; Crimson Spiral and Mountain's Will add 24 + 8 pierce on demand. The bleed engine (Vein Splitter snowball + Razor Cadence) is an independent armor-proof damage clock that keeps ticking through every shield refresh, and Reckless/Bloodprice bank the rage that turns on Crimson Spiral. Cheap physical front-load builds rage and resources before the dual-resource detonators come up. Same 14-card band as naive — pure sequencing + synergy delta.

decks.label: Naive air/earth control warrior vs doom_knight (boss1)
decks.notes: NAIVE: on-theme (Concussive Smash, Tempest Pike, Galekick, Quake all apply slow/stun) but there is NO slow detonator and no stun payoff — the slows merely throttle the enemy's cooldown passively while the player tries to win by raw physical damage (Mountain's Will, Triple Slash, Jab). Concussive Smash's 2-stun is never followed by a stun-conditional hit. Includes two off-theme/dead picks: Shield Bash (t2-attack-defense scales off armor this deck never stacks) and Guard (t1-defense, pure armor that converts to nothing). Card ORDER is bad: the 2-stamina Concussive Smash sits in slot 1 before any stamina regen, and the cheap slow opener Galekick is buried at slot 5.

decks.label: Optimized air/earth control warrior vs doom_knight (boss1)
decks.notes: OPTIMIZED: deliberate slow-detonate combo. Front-loads cheap slow appliers in fixed order — Galekick (slot1, 7x2+2slow), Quake (slot2, 1m 4slow), Tempest Pike (slot3, 2slow) — to bank a large slow pool FAST, then Concussive Smash (slot4) adds 3 slow + 2 stun, and Thunderstrike Catalyst (slot5, t3-air-attack-counter) DETONATES the whole pool for 6 Pierce per slow consumed. Stormstone Tempo and Static Skirmish (t3-agility-air-counter, 3 Pierce per slow) are secondary slow payoffs that also re-seed slow, so the engine never wastes a stack. The 2-stun from Concussive plus throttle keeps doom_knight's enrage from ever ramping. All stamina-leaning, snappy CDs, Granitewrath as the physical closer.

decks.label: Naive air/earth control warrior vs iron_golem (boss2)
decks.notes: NAIVE: control-flavored (Concussive Smash, Tempest Pike, Galekick, Quake apply slow/stun) but un-synergized vs iron_golem. No slow detonator at all, so against a 400HP/def4 shield-tank the passive cooldown-throttle does nothing to break the armor ratchet — wins (or loses) on raw STR damage. Includes Stormrage (t3-air-air-counter) which needs rage to fire but this deck builds no rage, so it is a dead card. Plus the same two filler dead picks (Shield Bash, Guard). Plain ascending order, expensive Concussive in slot 1.

decks.label: Optimized air/earth control warrior vs iron_golem (boss2)
decks.notes: OPTIMIZED: stacks slow early then detonates twice. Galekick -> Quake -> Tempest Pike -> Stormstone Tempo build a deep slow pool before Concussive Smash (stun+slow) and Thunderstrike Catalyst (slot6) detonate it for 6 Pierce/slow — Pierce ignores iron_golem's def4 + shield ratchet, the exact answer to a shield-tank. Static Skirmish and Sandfury (t3-air-earth-fire, 2 Pierce/slow) are second/third slow payoffs that re-detonate the re-seeded pool. The 2-stun freezes the golem's shield-cast window. Synergy = slow appliers front-loaded + Pierce detonators that bypass armor; the naive twin has the same appliers but converts none of them.

decks.label: Naive air/earth control warrior vs lizard_king (boss3)
decks.notes: NAIVE: theme-appropriate slow/stun applies (Concussive, Tempest Pike, Galekick, Quake, Static Bleed) plus physical hitters, but zero conversion of the control into damage. Vs lizard_king (370HP, multi_hit + 0.3 enrage) the right plan is to STUN-lock through the enrage; this deck applies stun once (Concussive) and never exploits it. No slow detonator, dead Stormrage (no rage), and the off-theme Shield Bash/Guard remain. Unordered: big 2-stam cards up front, cheap openers scattered.

decks.label: Optimized air/earth control warrior vs lizard_king (boss3)
decks.notes: OPTIMIZED: full slow-detonate engine plus a stun-lock vs lizard_king's enrage. Cheap appliers (Galekick, Quake, Tempest Pike, Stormstone Tempo) bank slow; Concussive Smash lands 2 stun + 3 slow; Thunderstrike Catalyst detonates 6 Pierce/slow; Static Skirmish and Sandfury are the follow-up Pierce-per-slow payoffs. Stormstone Tempo's stun-conditional bonus Pierce and Concussive's stun freeze the boss's attack-speed enrage so it never spikes. Mountain's Will + Granitewrath are the physical finishers once the pool is spent. The detonators + stun timing are exactly what the naive twin lacks.

decks.label: Naive air/earth control warrior vs desert_golem (boss5)
decks.notes: NAIVE: lots of slow/stun flavor (Concussive, Tempest Pike, Galekick, Quake, Static Bleed, plus Gale Echo t3-agility-air-air which doubles slow application) but NO detonator to cash the huge slow pool, so vs desert_golem (450HP/def4, big 10-shield every 7s) the slows just sit there while def4+shield eats all the non-Pierce physical damage. Gale Echo amplifies a pool that is never consumed — pure waste. Dead Stormrage (no rage) and off-theme Shield Bash/Guard included. Poor ordering, no early stamina-cheap opener.

decks.label: Optimized air/earth control warrior vs desert_golem (boss5)
decks.notes: OPTIMIZED: built to break a shield-tank with Pierce-per-slow. Galekick/Quake/Tempest Pike/Stormstone Tempo front-load slow; Gale Echo (t3-agility-air-air) then DOUBLES every subsequent slow application so the pool balloons; Thunderstrike Catalyst (6 Pierce/slow), Static Skirmish (3 Pierce/slow) and Sandfury (2 Pierce/slow) each detonate that amplified pool with armor-ignoring Pierce — the one line that beats desert_golem's def4 + 10-shield. Concussive's 2-stun is timed to skip a shield cast. Same appliers as the naive deck, but here Gale Echo amplifies and three detonators cash the pool instead of letting it rot.

decks.label: Naive air/earth control warrior vs boss_iron_golem (boss7)
decks.notes: NAIVE: heavy slow/stun theming (Concussive, Tempest Pike, Galekick, Quake, Static Bleed, Galebrand, Gale Echo) against the toughest tank (boss_iron_golem 470HP/def4, 12-shield every 7s, 0.35 enrage) but still NO slow detonator and NO stun finisher. The enormous slow pool that Gale Echo builds is never consumed; def4 + the 12 shield negate the raw STR damage; the late enrage then races the deck down. Dead Stormrage, off-theme Shield Bash/Guard, and a buried-opener ordering make it strictly a damage-race the deck cannot win.

decks.label: Optimized air/earth control warrior vs boss_iron_golem (boss7)
decks.notes: OPTIMIZED: peak slow-stun-detonate package for the hardest tank. Front-load Galekick/Quake/Tempest Pike/Stormstone Tempo to bank slow, Gale Echo doubles further applications, then Thunderstrike Catalyst + Static Skirmish + Sandfury chain-detonate the amplified pool as Pierce that ignores def4 and the 12 shield. The capstone is Tectonic Reckoning (t3-air-counter-earth, slot12): 3 stun + 50 STR Pierce — a hard-control nuke that both freezes the 0.35 enrage window and burst-clears the final HP. Sequencing matters: appliers -> Echo amplify -> Pierce detonators -> Tectonic finisher. The naive twin has the same appliers and Gale Echo but converts NONE of the pool and lacks the Tectonic stun-finisher, so it just loses the race.

decks.label: Pure poison (naive) vs doom_knight
decks.notes: Average loop-10 poison deck: it stacks poison via Mire Bloom + a grab-bag of 1m T3 appliers (Mireglide, Alchemic Drain, Stormsplash, Boilstep) and waits for the DoT. CRITICAL MISS: NO Bog Catalyst doubler and NO detonator anywhere, so poison never gets the quadratic spike that defines the archetype. Worse, three slots are diluted off-theme: Brine Bedrock and Alchemic Drain spend their budget on self-heal, Tidefoot Guard on armor, and t1-water/t1-earth/t1-fire are filler that apply zero or trivial poison. With doom_knight enraging at 50% HP, the slow raw-DoT clock risks a timeout/death.

decks.label: Pure poison (optimized) vs doom_knight
decks.notes: Master build sequenced for the full poison combo: cheap front-loaded appliers (Mire Bloom 1m, Stormsplash 1m, Slipstream 1m) ramp the stack fast and cheap; Slipvenom Tempo (peak 68.75, +5 bonus past 10 stacks) and Venom Dance (Vengeance +5) push stacks toward the chunk-cap; Bog Catalyst (t3-earth-earth-water) DOUBLES the accumulated poison for the quadratic spike; Steaming Plague spreads/tops up; then Drowning Lance (t3-attack-water-water) DETONATES, consuming half the pool into a Pierce burst that ignores doom_knight's def 3 and beats the 50% enrage clock. Every card is on-theme poison — no heal/armor dilution, no filler T1s.

decks.label: Pure poison (naive) vs iron_golem
decks.notes: On-theme but defensively-minded average deck. It leans into the armor/heal poison hybrids (Tidefoot Guard, Mistplate, Brineward, Bogplate, Brine Bedrock) so a lot of card budget is spent on survival instead of stacking poison, and it still has NO doubler and NO detonator. Against iron_golem (400 HP, +4 shield every 8s) raw DoT alone barely out-paces the shield regen, so this build trends toward a timeout — it survives but cannot close. Filler T1s (water/earth/fire) further slow the poison ramp.

decks.label: Pure poison (optimized) vs iron_golem
decks.notes: Built to punish iron_golem's def 4 + shield specifically: poison is DoT and ignores armor/shield entirely, so the win-condition routes around the boss's only defense. Front-loaded cheap 1m appliers ramp stacks, Slipvenom Tempo + Venom Dance spike them, Bog Catalyst DOUBLES, Steaming Plague spreads, then a DOUBLE-detonator package — Drowning Lance (single-target Pierce) and Marsh Squall (AoE, consume-half + reapply) — cashes the pool twice for two burst windows, out-racing the +4/8s shield. Tight elemental curve sustains on the 60-mana pool; zero defensive dead weight.

decks.label: Pure poison (naive) vs lizard_king
decks.notes: A typical loop-30 poison pile: lots of poison appliers but assembled with no plan. It mixes self-heal poison (Alchemic Drain, Brine Bedrock), armor poison (Tidefoot Guard, Mistplate, Brineward, Bogplate) and three filler T1s, so effective stacking is thin and there is STILL no Bog Catalyst doubler and no detonator. Against lizard_king (multi_hit x2, enrage at 30%) the deck has no burst to push through the enrage threshold quickly — it relies on a slow DoT tick that the enrage race can out-damage. Card order is unconsidered.

decks.label: Pure poison (optimized) vs lizard_king
decks.notes: Sequenced to race lizard_king's 30% enrage: dump a big poison stack FAST with cheap 1m appliers + Slipvenom Tempo (+5 past 10) and Venom Dance (Vengeance +5), then Bog Catalyst DOUBLES the pile into a near-cap quadratic load, Steaming Plague tops it up, and the Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall detonators convert the stack into immediate Pierce damage to blow past the enrage window before lizard_king's multi_hit out-trades the hero. One Mireglide late gives a little armor only as insurance — the rest is pure poison engine, properly ordered applier -> doubler -> spread -> detonate.

decks.label: Pure poison (naive) vs desert_golem
decks.notes: Average deep-run poison deck that wrongly tries to out-tank desert_golem (450 HP, +10 shield every 7s) with the armor/heal poison hybrids (Tidefoot Guard, Mistplate, Brineward, Bogplate, Brine Bedrock). With no doubler and no detonator, its poison output is a slow flat tick; the +10/7s shield regen plus the 450 HP pool means raw DoT cannot close — this is a near-certain timeout. The defensive cards keep it alive but with no win condition, exactly the off-curve trap the archetype falls into without synergy.

decks.label: Pure poison (optimized) vs desert_golem
decks.notes: The poison archetype's ideal answer to a shield tank: DoT ignores desert_golem's def 4 AND its +10/7s shield, so the win-condition bypasses everything the boss does. Aggressive front-loaded ramp (cheap 1m appliers + Slipvenom Tempo + Venom Dance) reaches double-digit stacks, Bog Catalyst DOUBLES to the chunk-cap, Steaming Plague spreads/refreshes, and the twin detonators (Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall) each consume HALF the pool for Pierce bursts that the shield can't absorb. Two healing/armor cards (Mireglide, Brine Bedrock) buy time against the 10-dmg hits without diluting the kill clock — survival in service of the poison payoff, not instead of it.

decks.label: Pure poison (naive) vs boss_iron_golem
decks.notes: End-game average poison deck, still built the wrong way: it piles on the armor/heal poison hybrids (Tidefoot Guard, Mistplate, Brineward, Bogplate, Brine Bedrock) to survive boss_iron_golem (470 HP, +12 shield/7s, enrage at 35%) but again has NO doubler and NO detonator, so its poison is a slow flat tick that the +12/7s shield largely cancels. Three filler T1s waste end-game slots. This is the headline failure case for unsynergized poison at depth: it can stall but never assemble lethal — a timeout.

decks.label: Pure poison (optimized) vs boss_iron_golem
decks.notes: Full end-game poison combo tuned to defeat boss_iron_golem's def 4 + 12/7s shield, both of which poison DoT ignores. Cheap 1m appliers front-load the ramp, Slipvenom Tempo (+5 past 10 stacks) and Venom Dance (Vengeance +5) drive stacks toward the chunk-cap, Bog Catalyst DOUBLES for the quadratic explosion, Steaming Plague spreads/refreshes, and the dual detonators (Drowning Lance single-target Pierce + Marsh Squall AoE consume-half) cash the pool in two burst windows that out-pace shield regen and clear the boss before its 35% enrage compounds. Mireglide + Brine Bedrock provide just enough armor/heal to weather the 12-dmg cadence — the entire deck is the poison engine, correctly ordered applier -> stacker -> doubler -> spread -> detonate, versus the naive build's defensive sprawl with no payoff.

decks.label: Naive Burn vs Doom Knight
decks.notes: Average player's 'fire deck': it applies burn (Spark, Flame Dart, Kindle, Cinderscar, Wickfencer, Slag Maul) and adds direct fire-attack damage, but carries NO detonator and NO Pyre Surge doubler, so the banked burn never gets cashed into a burst -- it only ticks at the soft-capped per-tick rate (8+floor((n-8)/2)) against a 380 HP boss, which is far too slow. It even wastes a slot on Mend (heal, off-theme) and Gust (free haste, no burn). Order is whatever the player picked up; the two big stamina T3s (Wickfencer/Slag Maul) sit mid-deck so on a 60-mana/30-stam mage they often stall on stamina. This is the textbook 'burn deck with no detonator' anti-pattern.

decks.label: Optimized Burn Detonate vs Doom Knight
decks.notes: Deliberate bank-and-cash loop: cheap reliable appliers are front-loaded (Spark 1m/cd1.2 x3, Flame Dart 1m/cd1.2 x2, Twinflame Flicker 1s/cd1.6 = +4 burn) to stack the burn bank fast on a mana-rich mage. Pyre (1m/cd1.4) is the repeatable detonator that consumes-and-reapplies so the engine keeps cycling cash-outs in fixed order; Pyre Surge (slot 6) DOUBLES the banked burn and adds +2/tick right before Cinderlance (slot 7) which both deals 8x2 direct AND consumes the now-doubled pool for 3 pierce/stack -- the combo finisher. Every card is fire/burn and costs 1, so the mana pool (60) sustains the whole chain. Same size band (10) as the naive deck, so build quality is the only variable.

decks.label: Naive Burn vs Iron Golem
decks.notes: Wider fire pile for the 2nd boss but still UNSYNERGIZED: many burn appliers (Spark, Flame Dart x2, Kindle, Magma Welling's 8 burn, Slag Maul, Cinderscar) but the only thing resembling a payoff is Steam Surge's '+4 if enemy has burn' -- there is NO Supernova/Cinderlance/Pyre detonator and NO Pyre Surge doubler, so a huge banked pool just sits there ticking under the soft cap against iron_golem's armor-affinity ratchet. Two slots are pure off-theme survival (Mend heal, Phoenix Aura heal) and one is filler (Gust). Magma Welling and Slag Maul are 2-stamina on a 30-stam mage and sit deep, so they frequently can't be afforded. Reasonable-looking, not built.

decks.label: Optimized Burn Detonate vs Iron Golem
decks.notes: Two staged detonations against a high-HP armored boss. Phase 1 banks fast with cheap mana appliers (Spark x3, Flame Dart x3 at 1m/cd1.2, Twinflame +4) and trickles small Pyre cash-outs (slot 5) to chip while building. The win-condition is the slot 7-8 sequence: Pyre Surge DOUBLES the accumulated bank then Supernova (2s+1m, exhaust) consumes the entire doubled pool for 5(int) PIERCE per stack to bypass iron_golem's armor affinity -- the single biggest burst the archetype can produce. Cinderlance (slot 11) is the second pierce detonator that re-cashes whatever rebanks. Costs are 1m appliers + the two-resource finishers spaced so the mage's mana(60)/stamina(30) regen (+1/+1 per 4.5s) covers them in fixed order. Pierce is the deliberate counter to the armor affinity; the naive deck has none.

decks.label: Naive Burn vs Lizard King
decks.notes: Thirteen-card fire deck that keeps doing what the naive player always does: stack burn from a big spread of appliers (Spark x2, Flame Dart x2, Kindle, Cinderscar, Magma Welling 8 burn, Slag Maul, Wickfencer) plus a couple direct fire hits, with ZERO detonators and ZERO Pyre Surge. Against lizard_king's higher HP the soft-capped DoT realization (chunk-cap 60) simply cannot out-pace the boss, and the deck leans on heals (Mend, Phoenix Aura) and Gust to survive rather than to close. Multiple 2-stamina T3s (Magma Welling/Slag Maul/Wickfencer) clog a 30-stam mage. It is on-theme and looks scary but has no kill button.

decks.label: Optimized Burn Detonate vs Lizard King
decks.notes: Double-doubler burst engine. Front-loaded 1m appliers (Spark x3, Flame Dart x3, Twinflame +4) build a deep bank, with Pyre (slot 6) keeping pressure on. The combo is sequenced so a Pyre Surge precedes EACH big detonator: Pyre Surge (slot 7) doubles into Cinderlance (slot 8, 8x2 + 3 pierce/stack), then a second Pyre Surge (slot 12) doubles the rebanked pool into Supernova (slot 11, 5 int pierce/stack, exhaust) for the kill. Two amplify->cash cycles squeeze maximum value out of the quadratic bank against a fat boss; all appliers are 1-cost mana to keep the elemental pool flowing, finishers spaced for two-resource regen. Same 13-card band as naive; only sequencing/synergy differs.

decks.label: Naive Burn vs Desert Golem
decks.notes: A loop-50 player's fire grab-bag: lots of appliers (Spark x2, Flame Dart x2, Kindle, Cinderscar, Magma Welling 8, Slag Maul, Wickfencer, Quench Lance) and the Steam Surge 'burn bonus' chip, but STILL no Supernova/Cinderlance/Pyre detonator and no Pyre Surge doubler. Quench Lance's 'if you consume 10+ burn gain int' rider is dead because nothing in the deck consumes burn. Against desert_golem (earth affinity, deep HP at bossMult 1.2) the unrealized bank loses the race; the deck pads with heals (Mend, Phoenix Aura) and Gust. Heavy on 2-stamina T3s a mage struggles to pay. On-theme, fundamentally non-functional as a kill engine.

decks.label: Optimized Burn Detonate vs Desert Golem
decks.notes: Scaling-fed double detonation for a deep boss. Cheap 1m appliers front-load the bank (Spark x3, Flame Dart x3, Twinflame +4); Quench Lance (slot 7) is included specifically because its rider grants +int when you consume 10+ burn -- here it actually fires, juicing every later int-scaled detonator. Pyre cash-outs (slots 6,11) chip while banking. The win con is the doubler->detonator pairs: Pyre Surge (8) -> Cinderlance (9, pierce), then Pyre Surge (12) -> Supernova (13, 5 int pierce/stack, exhaust) to crack desert_golem's earth-affinity wall with PIERCE. All-fire, all low-cost, finishers spaced for two-resource regen on the 60/30 mage. Same 14-card band as the naive list; the only delta is that this one closes.

decks.label: Naive Burn vs Boss Iron Golem
decks.notes: Endgame fire pile, same naive disease at max size: every burn applier the player owns (Spark x2, Flame Dart x2, Kindle, Cinderscar, Magma Welling 8, Slag Maul, Wickfencer, Quench Lance, Twinflame) jammed together with chip (Steam Surge) and survival filler (Mend, Phoenix Aura, Gust) -- and not one detonator or doubler. Against boss_iron_golem (defense affinity, highest HP, bossMult 1.3) the armor ratchet eats the unrealized soft-capped DoT entirely; there is no pierce, no burst, no cash-out. Stuffed with 2-stamina T3s that a 30-stam mage repeatedly skips. This is precisely the deck that 'looks like a burn deck' but mechanically cannot win the hardest fight.

decks.label: Optimized Burn Detonate vs Boss Iron Golem
decks.notes: Maximal bank-and-cash against the tankiest boss. Deep 1m applier base (Spark x4, Flame Dart x3, Twinflame +4) builds the largest possible quadratic bank; Quench Lance (slot 7) fires its consume-10+-burn rider to compound int into the int-scaled detonators. Two Pyre cash-outs (slots 6,11) maintain tempo. The closers are two amplify->PIERCE-detonator pairs aimed straight at boss_iron_golem's defense affinity: Pyre Surge (9)->Cinderlance (10, 8x2 + 3 pierce/stack) for the first crack, then Pyre Surge (13)->Supernova (14, 5 int pierce/stack, exhaust) for the kill -- pierce is the whole point, since raw DoT would be eaten by the armor ratchet. Every card is fire and 1-cost except the spaced two-resource finishers, sustaining on the 60/30 mage pool in fixed order. Same 15-card cap as the naive deck, isolating mastery as the variable.

decks.label: Boss1 Naive Poison+Burn (no payoff)
decks.notes: On-theme but UNSYNERGIZED: stacks poison appliers (Mire Bloom, Alchemic Drain, Venom Dance, Mireglide) and burn appliers (Spark, Flame Dart, Pyre) with ZERO conversion. No Bog-Catalyst doubler, no Pyre-Surge amplifier, no detonator — so the 6-stack cap silently wastes overflow and the DoT never gets cashed into burst. Off-theme filler: t1-water Mend (panic heal a real player tosses in). Plain order. Sim: still wins (avg player beats easy first boss) but slow at 21.0s TTK, 14 cards played, 62% HP — bleeds time vs the doom_knight.

decks.label: Boss1 Optimized Poison+Burn (doubler+detonators)
decks.notes: Full hybrid combo, front-loaded: Spark+Flame Dart cheap 1-cd burn openers, Mire Bloom + Venom Dance pre-load poison, then Bog Catalyst (t3-earth-earth-water) DOUBLES the poison pool, Pyre Surge (t3-air-fire-fire) DOUBLES burn and adds +2/tick, Cinder Sprint (t3-agility-fire-fire) and Venom Detonation (t3-counter-fire-water) DETONATE the inflated burn for burst, Pyre cashes leftover burn. Same 9-card band as naive but converts stacks instead of capping them. Sim: 15.0s TTK (29% faster), only 9 cards to close — mastery = speed, not win/loss.

decks.label: Boss2 Naive Poison+Burn (no payoff)
decks.notes: More appliers, still no engine: adds Stormsplash (t3-agility-air-water) and Steam Surge (t2-fire-water) but NO doubler/amplifier/detonator and NO pierce — a real problem vs iron_golem's armor-affinity ratchet, since raw DoT appliers and small unpierced hits get eaten by armor. Off-theme: t1-water heal. Sim: wins at 21.0s, 14 cards, but leaves the armor untouched and relies purely on DoT ticking through.

decks.label: Boss2 Optimized Poison+Burn (doubler+pierce detonator)
decks.notes: Adds the answer to iron_golem's armor: Slipvenom Tempo (t3-agility-water-water, premium 5+5 poison applier) feeds Bog Catalyst's double, then Drowning Lance (t3-attack-water-water) consumes half the doubled poison and deals PIERCE per stack — bypassing the armor ratchet entirely. Burn line (Flame Dart -> Pyre Surge -> Cinder Sprint) runs in parallel. Sequencing: appliers before doubler before detonator. Sim: 13.0s vs naive 21.0s (38% faster), 8 cards — the pierce detonator is what the naive deck structurally cannot do.

decks.label: Boss3 Naive Poison+Burn (no payoff)
decks.notes: 12-card pile of DoT appliers: Boilstep (t3-agility-fire-water) added as another dual applier, but it's just MORE application into the same 6-stack cap with no doubler/amplifier/detonator to spend it. Off-theme: t1-water heal. The deck wins by attrition (DoT eventually outpaces 388 HP) rather than by any combo. Sim: 17.0s, 12 cards.

decks.label: Boss3 Optimized Poison+Burn (full combo)
decks.notes: Mature engine: cheap openers -> Venom Dance + Slipvenom Tempo + Boilstep load both DoTs -> Bog Catalyst doubles poison -> Pyre Surge doubles burn -> Drowning Lance (pierce) and Cinder Sprint + Venom Detonation cash the pools. Boilstep (t3-agility-fire-water) is the glue card that seeds BOTH burn and poison in one 1-mana play so both detonator lines stay fed. Sim: 12.0s vs naive 17.0s, 76% HP, 8 cards — tighter and faster.

decks.label: Boss5 Naive Poison+Burn (no payoff)
decks.notes: 13 cards, deeper but still engine-less. Adds Mistplate (t3-air-defense-water) for armor+poison — a defensive off-theme pick a cautious player slots vs desert_golem. Loads lots of poison/burn but never doubles or detonates; relies on slow DoT attrition against 495 HP. Sim: wins comfortably (86% HP) but the slowest in the set at 21.0s, 14 cards — pure grind.

decks.label: Boss5 Optimized Poison+Burn (full combo)
decks.notes: 13-card mastery build: same poison+burn engine plus Steaming Plague (t3-air-fire-water), a premium poison applier (3 + 2 more if poison present) that turbo-feeds Bog Catalyst -> Drowning Lance pierce burst, while Pyre Surge -> Cinder Sprint + Venom Detonation handle burn. Every slot is an applier, doubler, amplifier, or detonator — no dead defensive card. Sim: 12.9s vs naive 21.0s (39% faster), 555 dmg in 9 cards.

decks.label: Boss7 Naive Poison+Burn (no payoff)
decks.notes: 14-card endgame pile, still no doubler/amplifier/detonator. Two defensive off-theme cards (Mistplate, Brineward t3-defense-water-water for armor+heal+poison) that an average player adds for safety vs boss_iron_golem but which dilute pressure. Against the boss's heavy armor the unpierced DoT-only plan is structurally weak — it wins only because DoT ignores armor, but slowly. Sim: 21.0s, 14 cards, 82% HP.

decks.label: Boss7 Optimized Poison+Burn (full combo + pierce)
decks.notes: 14-card peak build tuned for the armored boss_iron_golem: heavy poison generation (Venom Dance, Slipvenom Tempo, Steaming Plague, Boilstep, Marsh Squall t3-air-earth-water) all funnel into Bog Catalyst's double, then Drowning Lance AND Marsh Squall (both consume-half-poison PIERCE detonators) double-tap the inflated pool through armor; Pyre Surge -> Cinder Sprint + Venom Detonation run the burn payoff. Zero defensive filler — every card advances the kill. Sim: 13.0s vs naive 21.0s (38% faster), 8 cards. The two pierce detonators are the decisive mastery edge the naive deck lacks.

decks.label: Boss1 Mage Slow/Stun Control (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: a normal player throws together every card that says slow/stun, but builds NO payoff. It applies slow (Quake, Tempestbleed) and stun (Frostbind, Tremor Lock, Bedrock Snare) but has ZERO detonator/finisher to convert the control into damage. Tremor Lock and Bedrock Snare are mostly DEAD because nothing reliably opens the stun they gate on (Frostbind only lands ~1 stun early). The deck just throttles the boss and tries to chip with Spark/Tempestbleed/Mist Step healing -- against doom_knight's 380 HP this stalls and risks timeout. Includes Mend + Mist Step as off-theme survival padding a real player adds when unsure. No INT scaling thought; cards scattered with no opener-first logic.

decks.label: Boss1 Mage Slow/Stun Control (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: a deliberate slow-stack -> amplify -> DETONATE engine. Front-loads the cheapest slow appliers (Quake 1m cd1.4, Static Bleed 4 slow 1s cd1.8) FIRST so slow stacks fast and reliably in fixed order. Gale Echo (slow doubler) is slotted right after the appliers so every subsequent slow application echoes a bonus slow -- the synergy core the naive deck lacks. Then the win-cons fire in sequence: Thunderstrike Catalyst (consume all slow, 6 dmg/slow) and Static Skirmish (3 dmg/slow) cash the built-up slow pool into a burst; Cinder Squall converts a 5+ slow pool into a stun + AoE damage. Tremor Lock now has a guaranteed stun to exploit. Sandfury closes as a flat slow-scaling finisher. Every control stack has a payoff -- the entire point the naive build misses.

decks.label: Boss2 Mage Slow/Stun Control (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: same control-without-payoff trap, scaled up for iron_golem (400 HP, attack-affinity armor ratchet). Stacks slow/stun (Quake, Frostbind, Tremor Lock, Bedrock Snare, Dust Plague aura, Dustward) and leans on armor/heal padding (Mireglide, Mist Step, Misting Veil) but has NO slow detonator and NO stun nuke -- so the control never becomes lethal. Dust Plague and Dustward apply slow over time but nothing consumes it. Against a boss this just delays its turns while the player slowly chips; high timeout risk. Card order is whatever-came-to-hand, not opener-first.

decks.label: Boss2 Mage Slow/Stun Control (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: slow-engine -> amplify -> multi-detonator, tuned for a tankier boss. Cheap appliers first (double Quake + Static Bleed) build the pool; Gale Echo doubles every future slow application. The kill comes from STACKED payoffs the naive deck never assembles: Cinder Squall (5+ slow -> stun + 2 dmg/slow AoE), Thunderstrike Catalyst (consume all slow, 6 dmg/slow), Static Skirmish (3 dmg/slow), and Stormstone Tempo (3 hits each applying slow, +pierce if enemy stunned). Glacial Pact adds slow + armor to survive the golem while the engine ramps. Tremor Lock fires off a guaranteed stun. Sequencing is appliers -> amplifier -> detonators, so each detonation hits a maxed slow pool.

decks.label: Boss3 Mage Slow/Stun Control (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: control pile vs lizard_king (370 HP). Lots of slow/stun sources (Quake, Frostbind, Tremor Lock, Bedrock Snare, Dust Plague, Dustward, Stoneward Reprisal) plus armor/heal padding, but the player STILL never adds a slow detonator or stun nuke, so the boss is throttled but not killed quickly. Stoneward Reprisal's brace-stun and Tremor Lock's stun-gate often whiff because the deck can't guarantee the setup. This is the canonical 'I have all the control pieces but no win condition' mistake -- expect long TTK / timeout pressure. Order is unsequenced.

decks.label: Boss3 Mage Slow/Stun Control (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: full control-detonation engine plus a top-end finisher. Appliers + Gale Echo amplifier front-loaded exactly as the lower stages, so the slow pool maxes fast. The damage chain is layered: Cinder Squall (stun + AoE at 5+ slow), Thunderstrike Catalyst and Static Skirmish (both scale per slow), Sandfury (pierce per slow). The build-around capstone the naive deck lacks entirely is Tectonic Reckoning (3 stun + 50 pierce) -- it's the deliberate kill button placed late once mana banks. Glacial Pact buys survival. Tremor Lock now has reliable stuns to convert. Sequencing turns every accumulated slow stack into damage; the naive version converts none.

decks.label: Boss5 Mage Slow/Stun Control (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: deep control pile vs desert_golem (450 HP, earth-affinity). The player keeps adding slow/stun and survival cards (Quake, Frostbind, Tremor Lock, Bedrock Snare, Dust Plague, Dustward, Stoneward Reprisal, plus poison/heal armor padding from Mireglide/Bogplate/Mist Step) but still has NO finisher that converts control to damage. Against 450 HP this is the worst version of the trap: the deck out-survives the boss but cannot close, almost certainly timing out. Off-theme poison splashes dilute the slow count instead of feeding a detonator. No sequencing.

decks.label: Boss5 Mage Slow/Stun Control (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: maxed slow-stack -> Gale-Echo amplify -> multi-detonator burst, sized for a 450 HP boss. Two Quakes + Static Bleed open the slow pool; Gale Echo doubles all subsequent slow. Cinder Squall converts to stun+AoE; Thunderstrike Catalyst, Static Skirmish, and Sandfury each cash the (now-huge) slow pool for damage; Dust Plague's recurring slow keeps the pool topped between detonations. Tectonic Reckoning is the late nuke (3 stun + 50 pierce) that closes against the golem's HP. Glacial Pact + Tremor Lock provide survival and a stun-gate payoff. Same control pieces as the naive deck, but every stack now has a damage outlet and the order is opener->amplifier->detonator.

decks.label: Boss7 Mage Slow/Stun Control (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: the endgame version of the control-without-payoff trap vs boss_iron_golem (470 HP, defense-affinity, 12 dmg). The player has every control and survival tool (Quake, Frostbind, Tremor Lock, Bedrock Snare, Dust Plague, Dustward, Stoneward Reprisal, Bogplate, Mistplate, Mireglide) but no slow detonator and no stun nuke. With a 470 HP defense-affinity boss this cannot remotely close -- pure throttle, guaranteed timeout. Poison/armor splashes further dilute the slow stacks. This is the deck a mid-skill player brings and loses with despite 'doing everything right' defensively.

decks.label: Boss7 Mage Slow/Stun Control (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: peak control-detonation engine for the final boss. Identical opener logic -- double Quake + Static Bleed build slow, Gale Echo amplifies every application -- but the payoff layer is at its densest: Cinder Squall (stun + AoE/slow), Thunderstrike Catalyst (6 dmg/slow), Static Skirmish (3 dmg/slow), Sandfury (pierce/slow), Stormstone Tempo (slow hits + stun-pierce), Dust Plague (recurring slow refuel), and Tectonic Reckoning (3 stun + 50 pierce) as the closer. Galetide adds a pierce + slow + poison top-up. Against the 470 HP defense-affinity golem the Pierce-heavy detonators bypass its armor ratchet, and the stacked detonations against a maxed slow pool give the burst the naive deck can never produce. Pure sequencing + synergy is the only difference -- same control identity, opposite outcome.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Doom Knight (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: an on-theme heal+poison pile with NO poison synergy. It applies poison via Alchemic Drain / Venom Dance / Mire Bloom but has NO doubler (Bog Catalyst) and NO detonator (Drowning Lance/Marsh Squall), so poison only ever ticks linearly and never spikes. Heavy heal stacking (Mend, Tidesong, Misted/Squall Aura) keeps it alive but it is a pure race-loser with no second win condition, so vs Doom Knight (380HP, def3, enrage <50%) it tends to time out or slowly lose the DPS race. Plain non-sequenced order, one slightly-off card (Spark burn that nothing pays off).

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Doom Knight (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: the full poison combo line front-loaded. Open Quake (free-ish slow to throttle the boss cd), then stack poison fast (Alchemic Drain, Venom Dance), DOUBLE it with Bog Catalyst, then DETONATE with Drowning Lance (5 Pierce per poison consumed) for a real kill spike — the second win condition the naive deck lacks. Tidesong Aura ramps SPI (+2/cast) so the Mend/Misted/Squall heals grow, sustaining through enrage. Same size/cards-band as naive but ordered applier->doubler->detonator and control-first to survive the race.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Iron Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: more healing piled on for the tankier Iron Golem (400HP, def4, +4 shield/8s) but STILL no poison doubler and no detonator, and crucially no armor-pierce path. Plain poison appliers chip slowly while the golem's recurring shield soaks the small ticks; lots of heals (Tidesong, Misted, Squall, Brineward, Brine Bedrock, Bloodtide) keep it alive far past the clock -> timeout/loss. Off-theme filler heals crowd out a finisher.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Iron Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: built around poison that IGNORES the golem's def/shield via Pierce detonators. Sequence: Quake slow, then Steaming Plague + Venom Dance + Alchemic Drain to build a big poison pool, Bog Catalyst doubles it, then DOUBLE-detonate with Drowning Lance and Marsh Squall (Pierce per poison consumed) to bypass armor and shield - the win condition. Tidesong SPI ramp powers Mend + Misted heals to outlast the 10dmg hits. Same 12-card band as naive; the difference is doubler+detonator presence and applier-first ordering.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Lizard King (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: a deeper heal+poison stack for Lizard King (370HP, def3, multi-hit, enrage<30%). It even includes Slipvenom Tempo (a big poison applier with its OWN threshold doubler) but never reaches 10 poison reliably because there is NO Bog Catalyst doubler to prime it and NO detonator to cash the pool. So it just leaks linear poison while over-healing. Race-loser with no closer; the multi-hit + late enrage out-DPS the chip before it kills.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Lizard King (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: maximizes the poison engine. Steaming Plague + Venom Dance + Slipvenom Tempo build a large pool, Slipvenom's 10-poison threshold AND Bog Catalyst both double it, then Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall detonate for huge Pierce spikes - a real burst kill before enrage. Quake slow + the haste on Slipvenom/Misted manage the multi-hit pressure; Tidesong SPI ramp + Mend/Misted/Brine heals sustain. Same 13-card depth as naive but every poison applier is paired with a doubler and two detonators, and ordered build->double->pop.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Desert Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: late-game heal/poison/control grab-bag for Desert Golem (450HP, def4, +10 shield/7s). It splashes Cinder Squall for slow/stun control and lots of poison appliers, but with NO doubler and NO Pierce detonator the poison ticks are eaten by def4 + the big 10-shield, and the deck simply over-heals into a timeout. Control is present but uncoordinated (no Thunderstrike/Tectonic payoff), so the slow buys time it can't convert into a kill.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Desert Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: poison-Pierce + stacked control to beat def4/shield. Steaming Plague->Venom Dance->Slipvenom Tempo build, Bog Catalyst + Slipvenom threshold double, Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall detonate as Pierce (ignores def AND the 10-shield) - the win condition. Cinder Squall + Quake stack slow and Thunderstrike Catalyst CONSUMES the slow for a Pierce nuke (control payoff the naive deck never builds). Tidesong SPI ramp sustains the heals through the long fight. Same 14-card depth; difference is doubler+detonators+control-detonator and build-double-pop ordering.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Ancient Iron Golem (naive)
decks.notes: Naive: end-game heal/poison/control pile for Ancient Iron Golem (470HP, def4, +12 shield/7s, enrage<35%). Tons of sustain (Tidesong, Misted, Squall, Brineward, Brine Bedrock, Bloodtide, Mend) and scattered poison/slow, but again NO doubler, NO detonator, NO control payoff. Against def4 + a 12-shield ratchet the linear poison is almost fully absorbed; the deck heals forever and times out - the textbook race-loser with no second win condition at the hardest boss.

decks.label: Sustain/DoT vs Ancient Iron Golem (optimized)
decks.notes: Optimized: the complete sustain+poison-Pierce+control machine for the hardest boss. Build a huge poison pool (Steaming Plague, Venom Dance, Slipvenom Tempo), double via Bog Catalyst + Slipvenom's 10-stack threshold, then detonate as Pierce with Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall to bypass def4 and the 12-shield - the only way to convert chip into a kill here. Cinder Squall stacks slow->stun and Thunderstrike Catalyst pops the slow for a Pierce burst, freezing the golem's cd to survive enrage. Tidesong SPI ramp + Mend/Misted heals outlast the 12-dmg hits. Same 14-card band; the win comes purely from synergy (doubler + two poison detonators + a control detonator) and build-double-pop sequencing the naive deck lacks.

decks.label: Naive Poison Wall (boss1)
decks.notes: On-theme but UNSYNERGIZED: piles up armor+poison appliers and trusts poison ticks to wear down doom_knight. CRITICAL OMISSION: no Bog Catalyst doubler and no detonator (Drowning Lance / Marsh Squall), so the stack only ever climbs to ~6 and realizes as slow quadratic DoT under the chunk-cap; it never converts to burst pierce. Mire Bloom and the *plate cards each apply only 2-3 poison onto an already-near-cap stack, so most applications are wasted. Plain pickup order (Guard first as a generic armor reflex). Against doom_knight's low 6 dmg it survives, but leans on the timeout and may not close before the <50% enrage. Average-player 'poison + shields, good enough'.

decks.label: Bog-Catalyst Outlast (boss1)
decks.notes: SYNERGIZED applier->doubler->detonator line. Mireglide (1m 8armor+3poison+haste) and Tidefoot Guard (1m 12armor+2poison) open: both 1m so the deck always fires turn one, seeding poison while building the wall vs doom_knight's 6 dmg. Mire Bloom (1m) and Venom Dance (1s 3 poison / +5 on vengeance after the knight hits) ramp toward 10+ fast. Bog Catalyst (t3-earth-earth-water, 1s+1m) is the keystone the naive deck lacks: DOUBLES poison to the cap. Drowning Lance (t3-attack-water-water, 2s+1m) DETONATES half the pool as 5 Pierce/stack, bypassing def 3 and closing before the <50% enrage matters. Stagnant Bulwark (1s+1m) drips 2 poison/2s behind 10 armor to re-feed the doubler; Brineward (2m 14armor+heal12) anchors the outlast. Cheap 1m/1s cards front-loaded; both/2m cards trail. Same 10-card band as the naive build.

decks.label: Naive Shield-Soak Poison (boss2)
decks.notes: Average iron_golem answer: stack armor+poison, soak 10dmg/2.5s + 4-shield. UNSYNERGIZED in the way that loses here: NO doubler, NO detonator. iron_golem has 400 HP, def 4 AND a recurring +4 shield, so effective bulk is high; raw poison ticks (chunk-capped, never doubled, never burst-detonated) crawl, and the deck has almost no piercing damage, so the golem chips through armor while poison fails to close -> high timeout risk. Frostbind (1m stun+armor) is reasonable but off-theme and doesn't advance the poison plan. Most cards re-apply 2 poison onto a decaying stack = wasted. Plain order, Guard-first reflex.

decks.label: Pierce-Detonator Wall (boss2)
decks.notes: Built to beat iron_golem's def 4 + 4-shield via PIERCE. Mireglide + Tidefoot Guard (both 1m armor+poison) open the wall and seed poison vs 10dmg. Slipvenom Tempo (t3-agility-water-water, 1s+1m) is the big applier: 5 poison, +5 once the stack hits 10+, spiking past the cap. Bog Catalyst (1s+1m) DOUBLES the stack (the naive deck's missing keystone), then TWO detonators -- Drowning Lance (half pool as 5 Pierce/stack) and Marsh Squall (t3-air-earth-water, exhaust, half pool as 6 Pierce/stack AoE) -- convert the capped poison into burst Pierce that ignores both def and shield, which armor-based damage can't punch through. Stagnant Bulwark re-feeds poison behind armor; Brineward (2m heal12+armor) outlasts. 1m/1s appliers front-loaded; 2m anchor trails. Same 11-card band as the naive deck.

decks.label: Naive Outlast vs Lizard King (boss3)
decks.notes: Reasonable poison+armor pile vs lizard_king (370 HP, multi-hit x2, enrage <30%). UNSYNERGIZED: no doubler, no detonator. The flaw vs THIS boss: with no burst, the slow poison grind pushes the king below 30% where it ENRAGES to 1.5x speed with double hits, and the deck has no way to spike the last 30%, so it times out or gets overwhelmed during enrage. Steaming Plague (t3-air-fire-water) is a nice applier (+2 if poisoned) but without a doubler it never reaches the high stacks where poison's quadratic DoT pays off. Each *plate just re-applies 2 poison to a near-cap stack. Plain order.

decks.label: Detonate-Before-Enrage (boss3)
decks.notes: Race poison to the cap and DETONATE before lizard_king's <30% enrage. Mireglide + Tidefoot Guard open armor+poison vs the multi-hit. Steaming Plague (t3-air-fire-water, 1s+1m, 3 poison +2 if poisoned) and Slipvenom Tempo (5/+5) ramp hard. Bog Catalyst (1s+1m) DOUBLES to the cap -- the keystone the naive deck omits. Then Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall (two detonators) dump the whole pool as Pierce in one window, taking the king from a healthy bar straight through 30% so the enrage window is tiny; Pierce ignores its def 3. Stagnant Bulwark drips poison behind 10 armor; Brineward heals the multi-hit chip. Cheap appliers front, 2m anchor last. 11-card band matches the naive deck.

decks.label: Naive Desert Fortress (boss5)
decks.notes: Big armor+poison fortress vs desert_golem (450 HP, def 4, +10 shield/7s). On-theme but UNSYNERGIZED and this is where it breaks: the +10 shield every 7s plus def 4 eats almost all non-pierce damage, and the deck has NO detonator to turn poison into pierce and NO doubler to push poison into its high-quadratic band. Realized poison stays low while the shield keeps resetting -> times out against a 450 HP wall. Frostbind and Steam Bulwark (t3-defense-fire-water) are reasonable defensive picks but don't advance a win condition. Plain order, Guard-first. A normal player's 'stack more shields and poison' answer that can't close.

decks.label: Shield-Pierce Poison Fortress (boss5)
decks.notes: Maximizes PIERCE detonation through desert_golem's def 4 + recurring +10 shield, which blunts every non-pierce source. Mireglide + Tidefoot Guard open the armor wall vs 10dmg/2.7s. Slipvenom Tempo (5/+5) and Steaming Plague (3/+2) ramp the stack; Bog Catalyst (1s+1m) DOUBLES to the cap (naive deck's missing keystone); then Drowning Lance (5 Pierce/stack) and Marsh Squall (6 Pierce/stack AoE, exhaust) are TWO detonators that dump the capped pool as pure Pierce -- ignoring the shield and def entirely, the only reliable way through this boss. Stagnant Bulwark re-feeds poison behind 10 armor to reload the detonators; Brineward (2m heal12) + the *plate armor sustain the long fight. 1m/1s appliers front-loaded, 2m anchor late. 13-card band matches the naive deck.

decks.label: Naive Ancient-Golem Wall (boss7)
decks.notes: Endgame poison+armor pile vs boss_iron_golem (470 HP, def 4, +12 shield/7s, enrage <35%, DEFENSE affinity = armor ratchet). UNSYNERGIZED and the hardest test for it: defense affinity ratchets armor against the hero's hits, the +12 shield resets constantly, and def 4 eats chip -- so a no-detonator, no-doubler poison deck has essentially zero way to convert its capped poison into damage that lands. It armor-soaks then times out (or dies at the <35% enrage with no finisher). Stormsplash (t3-agility-air-water, poison+slow+burn) and Frostbind add diffuse control but no closer. Plain order. The realistic 'average' deck that cannot beat the final tank.

decks.label: Twin-Detonator Ancient Golem Killer (boss7)
decks.notes: Pure PIERCE-throughput plan vs boss_iron_golem's def 4 + 12-shield + defense-affinity armor ratchet -- the one wall where only pierce and only a doubled+detonated poison pool can win. Mireglide + Tidefoot Guard open armor vs 12dmg/2.5s. Triple applier ramp -- Slipvenom Tempo (5/+5), Steaming Plague (3/+2), Venom Dance (3/+5 on vengeance) -- floods poison to the cap fast; Bog Catalyst (1s+1m) DOUBLES it (the naive deck's missing keystone); then Drowning Lance (5 Pierce/stack) + Marsh Squall (6 Pierce/stack AoE) DETONATE the whole capped pool as Pierce, ignoring the shield, def 4, and the armor-affinity ratchet, spiking the boss through the <35% enrage threshold before its sped-up attacks matter. Stagnant Bulwark re-feeds poison behind 10 armor so detonators can reload; Brineward (2m heal12+armor) + Bogplate outlast. All cheap 1m/1s appliers front-loaded, 2m anchor last. 14-card band matches the naive deck so build quality is the only variable.

decks.label: Detonator/Control boss1 NAIVE
decks.notes: On-theme but UNSYNERGIZED. Applies poison (Mire Bloom, Slipstream) and burn (Spark, Flame Dart, Pyre) and a little slow/stun (Quake, Tremor Lock) but has NO poison doubler (no Bog Catalyst), NO poison detonator (no Marsh Squall/Drowning Lance), and NO burn amplify/detonator (no Pyre Surge/Supernova). DoTs just tick out raw, so the kill grinds: SIM win=1.00 but ttk=41.7s over 34 cards played, 50% HP — exactly an average player's plod. Mend/Frostbind/Tremor Lock are slightly-off filler that an average player includes 'to be safe' but that do nothing for the DoT plan. Plain card order: cheap appliers then random support.

decks.label: Detonator/Control boss1 OPTIMIZED
decks.notes: Deliberate combo + sequencing. Front-loads cheap reliable appliers (Spark 1m, Flame Dart 1m, Quake 1m, Venom Dance 1s) to bank a big DoT pool, THEN the multipliers fire in order: Bog Catalyst (t3-earth-earth-water) DOUBLES poison, Pyre Surge (t3-air-fire-fire) doubles+amplifies burn, and the detonators cash it all in — Marsh Squall (t3-air-earth-water) 6-Pierce/poison-stack and Supernova (t3-fire-fire-fire) 5-Pierce/burn-stack as the finisher. Slipvenom Tempo threshold-doubles poison past 10 stacks. Same archetype, same 10-card band as the naive deck, but ttk collapses to 15.0s in only 9 cards at 59% HP — ~2.8x faster purely from the doubler+detonator assembly the naive deck lacks.

decks.label: Detonator/Control boss2 NAIVE
decks.notes: Average build for iron_golem (400HP, attack-affinity). Lots of poison/burn appliers plus Steaming Plague (conditional poison) but still NO doubler and NO true detonator line — it relies on appliers stacking naturally. Includes Mend/Frostbind/Tremor Lock as panicky off-theme survival picks. Result: win=1.00 but ttk=25.0s / 18 cards, the slow grind of un-detonated DoTs against a high-HP boss.

decks.label: Detonator/Control boss2 OPTIMIZED
decks.notes: Full engine. Opener appliers (Spark, Flame Dart, Venom Dance, Quake) -> Steaming Plague stacks poison -> Bog Catalyst DOUBLES it -> Slipvenom Tempo threshold-doubles past 10 -> dual detonation: Marsh Squall (poison) and Supernova (burn), with Pyre Surge pre-amplifying burn. Thunderstrike Catalyst (t3-air-attack-counter) is the slow detonator that also keeps iron_golem's cd throttled (control buys time). Same 12-card band as naive; ttk drops to 15.6s / 10 cards. The win-condition is REACHABLE in fixed order because doublers sit right after the appliers and detonators right after the doublers.

decks.label: Detonator/Control boss3 NAIVE
decks.notes: 13-card mid-run pile vs lizard_king. Good poison/burn coverage and Alchemic Drain (t3-earth-fire-water) for sustain, but again NO Bog Catalyst doubler and NO detonator — DoTs are never cashed, so damage stays linear. Frostbind/Tremor Lock/Mend are off-curve survival filler. win=1.00, ttk=20.0s / 14 cards, dmg only 392.

decks.label: Detonator/Control boss3 OPTIMIZED
decks.notes: Same 13-card band, fully synergized. Appliers -> Steaming Plague + Slipvenom Tempo build a deep poison pool -> Bog Catalyst doubles -> Marsh Squall and Supernova detonate (burn pre-doubled by Pyre Surge). Cinder Squall (t3-air-air-fire) adds the slow->stun control payoff so lizard_king's attacks are throttled while DoTs detonate.
