Slipstream (1m pierce+poison) and Venom Dance (1s 3/+5 poison on vengeance) ramp the stack fast; Bog Catalyst doubles; Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall are the two detonators; Stagnant Bulwark and Brineward provide the only armor/heal so it must close quickly. Front-loaded 1m/1s appliers keep tempo high. SIM (6 reps vs bog_witch): winRate 1.0, hp 1.0, ttk 21s, 14 cards cycled, 0 deaths/timeouts.

decks.label: Endgame Poison Fortress (boss5)
decks.notes: Tuned vs desert_golem (450 HP, def 4, 10dmg/2.7s, +10 shield/7s). Two openers of cheap armor+poison (Mireglide, Tidefoot Guard), Alchemic Drain sustain, Bog Catalyst + Slipvenom Tempo to spike and re-spike the stack past 10, Stagnant Bulwark drip, then Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall detonators to punch through def/shield with pierce. Brineward/Brine Bedrock heal the long fight. Order keeps all 1m appliers ahead of the 2m walls. SIM (6 reps, desert_golem bossMult1.2, eHP495): winRate 1.0, hp 1.0, ttk 18s, dmg 522, 0 deaths/timeouts.

decks.label: Shield-Pierce Poison (boss5)
decks.notes: Maximizes pierce-detonation against desert_golem's def 4 + periodic shield, which blunts ordinary armor-based damage. Slipvenom Tempo big poison spike, Bog Catalyst doubler, then Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall convert the capped pool to pierce that ignores def/shield. Magma Welling and Stagnant Bulwark/Brineward/Mistplate hold the wall. 1m appliers lead, 2m anchors trail. SIM (6 reps vs desert_golem): winRate 1.0, hp 1.0, ttk 13s, dmg 528, 0 deaths/timeouts.

decks.label: Slow-detonator control vs iron_golem (Thunderstrike line)
decks.notes: Slow-control opener: cheap Quake (1m) then Stormsplash (1m, poison+slow+burn) and Cinder Squall (1s+1m, AoE slow that converts to stun + slow-scaled damage) build the slow pool every loop. Gale Echo (1s+1m) doubles every slow applied so the pool climbs fast. Thunderstrike Catalyst sits mid-deck as the slow detonator (consume all slow, 6 per slow Pierce) once 4-5 stacks exist. Quake/Spark/Stormsplash are 1-cost front-loaded so the deck reliably opens on a near-empty pool; the 1s+1m 'both' cards are interleaved so neither mana nor stamina starves. Stormsplash poison and Slipvenom add a second clock vs iron_golem's armor-affinity ratchet. Concussive Smash closes if the detonator under-delivers.

decks.label: Poison-detonator control vs iron_golem (Marsh Squall line)
decks.notes: Poison-stacking detonator: Stormsplash (1m) and Alchemic Drain (1m) open cheaply and seed poison; Bog Catalyst (1s+1m) doubles the poison pool; Slipvenom Tempo (1s+1m) and Steaming Plague (1s+1m) pile on volume since low mage INT means we lean on flat stacks. Marsh Squall (2s, exhaust) is the detonator — placed mid-deck after the doublers so it consumes a fat pool for 6 Pierce per poison AoE. Cinder Squall adds slow→stun control to survive iron_golem's hits while poison ticks. Cheap 1-cost Spark/Quake/Alchemic Drain front-loaded keeps the mana-heavy deck flowing under +1m/4.5s regen.

decks.label: Burn-detonator control vs lizard_king (Supernova line)
decks.notes: Burn bank-and-cash into Supernova: Spark (1m), Flame Dart (1m), Pyre (1m) front-load cheap burn application so the pool banks every loop. Pyre Surge (1s+1m) doubles the burn pool and adds +2/burn before the detonator. Supernova (2s+1m, exhaust) is the payoff — 5 INT-scaled Pierce per burn consumed to all enemies, placed after the doublers. Cinder Squall + Gale Echo + Dust Plague layer slow/stun control so the mage survives lizard_king's tempo while burn banks; Venom Detonation gives a second smaller detonate. Cheap 1m cards up front guarantee the open; 'both' cards interleaved to avoid mana stall.

decks.label: Poison/burn dual-detonator control vs lizard_king
decks.notes: Dual-DoT detonator: Stormsplash (1m), Boilstep (1m, poison+burn), Slipvenom (1s+1m), Steaming Plague (1s+1m) build both poison and burn pools cheaply up front. Venom Detonation (1s+1m) cashes the burn pool inline; Marsh Squall (2s, exhaust) cashes the bigger poison pool as AoE Pierce. Bog Catalyst doubles poison before the detonate. Gale Echo + Cinder Squall maintain slow/stun control so the mage buys time while two DoT clocks run. Front-loaded 1m appliers keep the open reliable; the 'both' cards are spaced so stamina (30) isn't drained by the back-to-back detonators.

decks.label: Poison-detonator control vs bog_witch (Marsh Squall, scaled)
decks.notes: Deep poison-detonator for L15. Heavy front of cheap appliers — Stormsplash (1m), Boilstep (1m), Slipvenom (1s+1m), Steaming Plague (1s+1m), Venom Dance (1s) — to outpace bog_witch's own poison/heal. TWO Bog Catalyst copies double the pool aggressively; Marsh Squall (2s, exhaust) detonates for huge AoE Pierce. Cinder Squall + Gale Echo + Dust Plague form the slow→stun control shell to freeze bog_witch's casts while poison ticks. Mostly T3 with no dead T2; mana-leaning with 1s/1m splashes front-loaded so the deck never opens empty under the slow regen.

decks.label: Slow-lock control + Thunderstrike vs bog_witch
decks.notes: Slow-lock variant: Quake (1m) and Stormsplash (1m) open, Cinder Squall + Gale Echo + Dust Plague stack slow every loop and convert overflow to stun (Gale Echo doubles each slow applied, Dust Plague tickers slow every 2.5s). Thunderstrike Catalyst is the slow detonator (6 Pierce per slow). Tremor Lock + Concussive Smash + Tectonic Reckoning (3s+2m, exhaust nuke) punish a stunned bog_witch with big hits. Slipvenom + Bog Catalyst + Steaming Plague provide a poison backup clock since pure slow has low raw damage at low INT. Cheap 1-cost openers guarantee the early slots fire.

decks.label: Poison-detonator control vs infernal_dragon (endgame poison)
decks.notes: Endgame (L18) poison-detonator. Front of cheap appliers — Stormsplash (1m), Boilstep (1m), Slipvenom (1s+1m), Steaming Plague (1s+1m), Venom Dance (1s) — generates a huge poison pool fast; two Bog Catalyst doublers compound it; Marsh Squall (2s, exhaust) detonates as 6 Pierce/poison AoE. Cinder Squall + Gale Echo + Dust Plague + Thunderstrike form a slow/stun shell to survive the dragon's heavy burn pressure. Pure-mana economy backed by 1s splashes; detonator + Thunderstrike are the only stamina sinks so the 30 pool sustains. Tests whether poison-detonate scales into the 1.25x boss / 1.5x normal band.

decks.label: Burn Supernova + control vs infernal_dragon
decks.notes: Burn-detonate at L18. Spark/Flame Dart/Pyre (all 1m) front-load burn banking; Pyre Surge (1s+1m) doubles the pool +2/burn; Venom Detonation cashes a smaller burn pool inline; Supernova (2s+1m, exhaust) is the big AoE burn detonator. Cinder Squall + Gale Echo + Dust Plague hold slow/stun control. A poison sub-engine (Stormsplash, Bog Catalyst, Slipvenom) gives a second clock because the dragon's HP is high enough that one burn dump may not close. Mana-heavy with the two exhaust 'both' detonators spaced apart so stamina recovers between them.

decks.label: All-DoT triple-detonator vs boss_iron_golem (final)
decks.notes: Final-boss (L19) all-DoT build vs the armor-affinity boss_iron_golem. Cheap appliers up front (Stormsplash 1m, Boilstep 1m, Slipvenom 1s+1m, Steaming Plague 1s+1m) seed poison+burn; two Bog Catalyst doublers compound poison; Venom Detonation cashes burn, Marsh Squall (2s) and Tremor Detonate (2s, exhaust) cash poison/burn/bleed as Pierce — Pierce matters because boss_iron_golem ratchets armor. Cinder Squall + Gale Echo + Dust Plague + Thunderstrike are the slow/stun control shell. Three detonators are spread across the deck so the 30 stamina pool can recover the 2s costs between casts.

decks.label: Slow-lock Thunderstrike + Tectonic finisher vs boss_iron_golem
decks.notes: Slow-lock finisher for the final boss. Quake (1m) + Stormsplash (1m) open; Cinder Squall + Gale Echo + Dust Plague stack slow and convert to stun (Gale Echo doubles each application, Dust Plague tickers it). Thunderstrike Catalyst detonates slow for 6 Pierce/stack; Tremor Lock + Concussive Smash hit a stunned target; Tectonic Reckoning (3s+2m, exhaust) is the all-or-nothing Pierce nuke that ignores the golem's armor ratchet while it's stun-locked. Poison backup (Slipvenom, Bog Catalyst, Steaming Plague, Venom Dance) supplies steady damage since slow itself deals none. Cheap 1m openers + spaced stamina sinks keep both pools alive against boss_iron_golem's 1.3x scaling.

decks.label: Loop8 Casual Burn Mess (no payoff)
decks.notes: Genuinely weak/messy loop-8 burn draft. Opens with cheap 1m Spark and free Gust (Gust is a dead card: pure haste, no damage), then Mend (heal = race-losing per C3). The burn 'payoff' Pyre sits in slot 7 and Magma Vein (2m, 2.3 CD) is buried last, so the deck applies burn faster than it ever cashes it; t2-air-fire (Firestorm, peakOPS 0 conditional aura) frequently whiffs. Quake (pure slow, no damage) is a dead slot. Low mage INT (~3) means the flat burn/spark numbers stay tiny. Curve is front-loadable but the win condition never assembles — should win normal loop-8 enemies only slowly.

decks.label: Loop8 Control-No-Kill (slow/stun, no win con)
decks.notes: A casual 'control' pile that can't actually kill. Quake (pure slow, no damage per C5) and Gust (free, dead) lead; the two payoff cards Tremor Lock and Bedrock Snare are both gated on the enemy already being stunned, which this deck rarely achieves, so they skip/whiff. Frostbind gives a little stun+armor but no scaling. Heal (Mend) and Misting Veil (peakOPS 2.57) are wasted tempo. Only real damage is one buried Spark dealing ~2. Demonstrates a weak mage with near-zero offense; likely timeouts on normals.

decks.label: Loop8 Mana-Hungry Stall (bad curve)
decks.notes: Curve-sabotage variant: leads with Magma Vein (2m, 2.3s CD) and Pyre back-to-back, two same-resource mana sinks that out-pace the +1 mana/4.5s regen, so the deck stalls and skips in the opening. Healing and Misting Veil dilute further. The cheap reliable cards (Spark, Flame Dart) that should be up front are buried at the back. Intentionally shows how a same-resource, expensive-first mana deck stalls out at loop 8.

decks.label: Boss1 Weak Burn Mage vs doom_knight (easy-check)
decks.notes: The headline doom_knight easy-check for a WEAK mage. Opens with cheap reliable burn (Spark, Flame Dart) then the Pyre detonator at slot 4, which is reasonable, but the deck is still soft: Gust (dead free card), Mend (heal, race-losing), Quake (pure slow no damage), and a slow 2.3s Magma Vein dilute the clock. Against doom_knight's 380 HP / def 3 / 9 dmg, low INT (~3) keeps burn payoffs small (Pyre deals 4 + ~3/burn) so the race is tight; this is the deck that tests whether an 'easy' first boss is genuinely beatable by a mediocre mage or punishingly slow.

decks.label: Boss1 Overhealer Mage (race-loser)
decks.notes: A casual 'I'll just outlast it' mage that violates the C3 race-loser principle in practice: three healing/defensive cards (Mend, Steam Surge heal mode, Misting Veil, Frostbind) up front, only thin burn for offense. Against doom_knight (9 dmg, 380 HP) the heals buy time but the deck lacks the damage to ever close, so it should trend toward timeouts/long TTK — a clean demonstration that defense without a second win condition can't beat even the easy first boss as a mage.

decks.label: Boss1 Splashy Off-Element Mage (incoherent)
decks.notes: Incoherent 'rainbow' draft a real casual player ends up with: dabbles in poison (Mire Bloom), burn (Spark, Pyre), and control (Bedrock Snare) without committing to any, plus DEX-scaled splashes (Gale Cut, Mist Step) that scale off the mage's low DEX. No archetype reaches critical mass, the lone poison source never doubles, and Pyre has almost no burn pool to consume. Front cards are cheap so it opens, but it has no real kill plan vs doom_knight — a representative weak/suboptimal mage that the easy-check should still ideally clear, slowly.

decks.label: Mixed elemental starter (loop5)
decks.notes: Fresh 6-card drafted toolbox. Opens with the two cheapest reliable 1m cards (Spark burn+dmg, Mend heal cushion) so the deck never stalls on empty mana. t2-fire-water (Steam Surge) is the workhorse: 6 dmg +4 vs burn, heal 4, refunds 1 stamina. t2-earth-water (Mire Bloom) is the perfect toolbox card: armor+heal+dmg+poison in one. t2-air-air (Tailwind) deals dmg, hastes, REFUNDS 1 mana to keep the cheap engine spinning. t1-earth (Quake slow) closes as soft control. Avg ~1m cost is within passive regen + Tailwind refund. INT~5 so flat values carry.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox w/ tempo splash (loop5)
decks.notes: Variant leaning on free/cheap tempo. t1-air (free Gust haste) opens for nothing so something always plays turn 1, then Spark (1m). t1-attack (Jab, 9 dmg, 1s) is a cheap stamina splash giving reliable on-curve damage while mana banks. Steam Surge + Mire Bloom are the dual-element core. t1-water heal sits mid-deck as the cushion. t2-agility-fire (Flame Dart, 1m, 5 dmg + 2 burn) closes. Interleaves stamina/mana/free so no two same-resource expensive cards stack back to back.

decks.label: Mixed elemental toolbox (loop8)
decks.notes: 9-card deck adding the first T3. Cheap Spark opener, then the Steam Surge/Mire Bloom/Tailwind cheap-mana core (Tailwind refunds mana to sustain). Flame Dart + Pyre (t2-fire-fire, burn detonate) build and cash burn. Quake adds slow. Closes with t3-earth-fire-water (Alchemic Drain, only 1m): poison + heal, an efficient cheap T3 that fits the toolbox. Front-loaded cheap cards keep it opening reliably; one true T3 at this stage is realistic.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox, poison-leaning (loop8)
decks.notes: 10-card variant tilting the toolbox toward poison+burn DoT while keeping heal/slow. Opens Spark+Mend, then Mire Bloom (poison) and Steam Surge (burn payoff). Tailwind sustains mana. Two cheap 1m T3s: Alchemic Drain (poison+heal) and Boilstep (t3-agility-fire-water: 6 Pierce + burn + poison). Quake/Flame Dart/Jab round out control + cheap filler at the back so the deck never bricks. Sustainable avg cost.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox vs doom_knight (boss1)
decks.notes: Loop-10 average drafted mage vs doom_knight (380 HP, def 3, 9 dmg). Cheap Spark opener, then the Steam Surge/Mire Bloom/Tailwind core for dmg+armor+heal+mana sustain. Burn line (Pyre, Flame Dart) plus poison T3s: Alchemic Drain (poison+heal), Boilstep (burn+poison), and t3-air-fire-water (Steaming Plague: 3->5 poison) for the unblockable kill. Mend + Mire Bloom + heals buy HP cushion the boss demands; Quake slows its hits. Beatable-but-not-trivial profile: DoT closes while heal/slow survive its 9-dmg swings.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox boss1 (control tilt)
decks.notes: Loop-10 average drafted mage vs doom_knight. Opens Spark + Mend for the HP cushion the boss demands, then Mire Bloom + Steam Surge for armor/heal/dmg. Tailwind sustains mana. Adds slow/stun control vs the boss's hard hits: t2-earth-earth, Quake, and t3-air-air-fire (Cinder Squall: slow->stun + dmg). t3-earth-earth-water (Bog Catalyst) doubles poison for the kill; t3-agility-air-water (Stormsplash) layers poison+slow+burn. Heal + slow buy time for the poison/burn DoT to close — the toolbox plan vs a beatable boss.

decks.label: Mixed elemental toolbox (loop15)
decks.notes: 13-card mid deck, mostly T3 with cheap T2 staples. Spark opener; Steam Surge/Mire Bloom/Tailwind cheap core sustains mana. Poison engine: Steaming Plague (3->5 poison), Bog Catalyst (double poison), Alchemic Drain + Boilstep (cheap poison+burn). Heal from Mend/Mire Bloom, slow/stun from Cinder Squall. t3-fire-fire-water (Quench Lance) ramps INT for the DoT. Cheap cards front and back so the deck opens and never bricks on mana; expensive 2m cards are absent to stay sustainable.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox vs iron_golem (boss2)
decks.notes: vs iron_golem (armor-ratchet boss). Poison ignores the armor affinity, so the toolbox tilts poison-heavy: Steaming Plague + Bog Catalyst (double) + Boilstep + Alchemic Drain stack DoT that bypasses the golem's defense. Cheap Spark/Mire Bloom/Steam Surge/Tailwind open and sustain. Slow/stun (earth-earth, Cinder Squall, Quake) throttle the golem's hits. t3-defense-water-water (Brineward) is the survival anchor: armor+heal+poison. Heal/slow buy time for the unblockable DoT to win.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox endgame (boss5)
decks.notes: 15-card forge-cap toolbox vs desert_golem (boss5, mult 1.2). Mostly T3. Poison is the unblockable backbone (Steaming Plague, Bog Catalyst double, Boilstep, Stormsplash, Alchemic Drain) with Quench Lance ramping INT to amplify every stack. Survival from t3-fire-water-water (Phoenix Aura: heal+burn+emergency armor) and t3-defense-water-water (Brineward: armor+heal+poison). Cheap Spark/Mire Bloom/Steam Surge/Tailwind/Mend open and sustain mana so the 1m / 1s+1m T3s never brick. Cinder Squall + Quake throttle the golem. Wins by out-DoTing through armor while heals outlast its damage.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox burn+poison (boss5)
decks.notes: 15-card boss5 variant that blends burn and poison rather than pure poison. Spark + Flame Dart + Pyre (t2-fire-fire) bank-and-cash burn; Quench Lance ramps INT to push both DoTs. Poison from Steaming Plague + Bog Catalyst double. Steam Surge/Mire Bloom/Tailwind keep the cheap-mana engine and refunds flowing. Phoenix Aura + Brineward provide the heal/armor survival layer, Cinder Squall the slow/stun. Cheap Spark/Steam Surge/Mire Bloom front-load tempo; the deck closes desert_golem on stacked DoT while heals tank its hits.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox endgame (boss6)
decks.notes: 15-card toolbox vs infernal_dragon (boss6, mult 1.25). Heavy poison core that ignores the dragon's pressure: Steaming Plague + Bog Catalyst double + Boilstep + Stormsplash, all amplified by Quench Lance INT ramp. Triple healing layer for the long fight: Phoenix Aura, Brineward, t3-earth-water-water (Brine Bedrock heal+poison) and t3-air-air-water (Squall Aura heal+mana+haste) which also refills mana to keep casting. Cheap Spark/Mire Bloom/Steam Surge/Tailwind front-load tempo. Plan: survive the burst with stacked heals while the unblockable poison DoT grinds the dragon down.

decks.label: Mixed toolbox vs boss_iron_golem (boss7)
decks.notes: 15-card final-boss toolbox vs boss_iron_golem (boss7, mult 1.3, armor ratchet). Poison answers the armor affinity since DoT bypasses it: Steaming Plague, Bog Catalyst double, Boilstep, Stormsplash, with Quench Lance pumping INT to grow every stack. Quadruple heal/sustain (Phoenix Aura, Brineward, Brine Bedrock, Squall Aura) outlasts the golem's escalating hits, and Squall Aura/Tailwind refund mana so the engine never stalls in a long fight. Cinder Squall slow/stun + Quake-equivalent throttle. Cheap Spark/Mire Bloom/Steam Surge open. Tuned endgame mixed deck: survive forever, win on unblockable poison.

decks.label: Warrior loop-2 basics (stamina opener)
decks.notes: Bedrock warrior opener at L2. Pure 1-stamina cards so passive regen (+1s/4.5s) sustains the chain; nothing back-to-back can stall. Order front-loads the two highest-OPS hits (Jab 9, Quickstep 4x2) for immediate tempo, then Guard for a survivability beat, Riposte aura for sustained chip, and Reckless Strike (T2, 1s, deals 9 + rage) as the only non-T1 to add a damage/rage ceiling. All stamina = no mana stall on a 50/30 pool.

decks.label: Warrior loop-2 with Gust tempo splash
decks.notes: All-T1 fresh warrior, demonstrates the free Gust (t1-air, cost free) as a no-resource haste enabler in slot 2 so it never stalls and speeds every later card. Jab opens, Gust hastes, then Quickstep/Guard/Riposte. Entirely stamina + one free card, ideal for a 50-stamina hero who has barely leveled (L2).

decks.label: Warrior loop-5 physical core
decks.notes: L5 warrior who has added a few cheap T2s. Front loads the reliable 1s T1 hits (Jab, Quickstep), then 1s T2 damage (Flurry Step 4+4, Bloodprice Strike 12+stam refund), with Guard as a defensive breather mid-deck. Bloodprice and Sidestep&Slash both refund stamina to keep the engine fed; Granite Lunge (attack+earth, 1.4cd) is the back-slot armor+damage closer. All stamina, sustainable average cost ~1.1s.

decks.label: Warrior loop-5 counter/bleed lean
decks.notes: Early bleed/rage warrior. Cheap 1s hits open (Jab, Quickstep, Reckless Strike for early bleed/rage), then Riposte (T1 aura) and Razor Stance (T2 bleed-on-hit aura) layered so the aura windows overlap the multi-hits, then Sidestep&Slash and Bloodprice apply bleed and refund stamina. All 1-stamina, low CD; auras placed after a hit so there is always something to trigger them.

decks.label: Warrior loop-5 sword-and-board
decks.notes: Defensive-leaning L5 warrior. Opens with cheap T1 offense then builds armor: Guard (12 armor), Parrying Stance (armor + stam refund + brace), Shield Bash (deals damage = armor, the payoff of the armor built), Tremor Dash (armor + 7 damage). Riposte aura closes. All 1-stamina except none over 1; Shield Bash intentionally sits after the armor-builders so it has armor to convert. Sustainable on 50 stamina.

decks.label: Mage loop-2 elemental basics
decks.notes: Bedrock mage opener at L2 (INT ~2, magic scaling weak so leans on flat values). Spark opens burn, free Gust hastes, Quake applies slow control, Mend heals for sustain, and Pyre (T2 fire, 1m) is the lone T2 that consumes the burn Spark/Pyre stack for a burst payoff. Almost all mana (1m each) on a 60-mana pool; one free card. Order puts the burn-applier before the burn-consumer.

decks.label: Mage loop-2 fire/air aggro splash
decks.notes: Fresh L2 mage tilted aggressive. Spark stacks burn first, free Gust hastes, Mend for a safety heal, then Flame Dart (agility+fire, 1m, 5 + 2 burn) as the T2 burn-builder, Quake for control. Cheap mana curve sustains on 60 mana; the one agility element is on a mana-cost card so no stamina stall. Burn appliers ordered before any payoff.

decks.label: Mage loop-5 burn engine
decks.notes: L5 burn mage. Spark opens the burn pool, free Gust hastes, Flame Dart and Pyre build/cash burn (Pyre consumes all burn for 3/stack), Mend heals, Firestorm (air+fire aura) banks 10 burn if 3+ cards played (always true here), Magma Vein (2m) is the only 2-cost, placed last so the deck never opens on it. Mana-heavy on 60 mana; burn-builders precede the Pyre cash slot.

decks.label: Mage loop-5 control/sustain
decks.notes: L5 control mage. Quake opens slow, free Gust hastes, Spark adds a burn tick, Mend sustains, then the T2 control core: Tremor Lock (earth+earth, stun/slow payoff if enemy stunned), Frostbind (water+water, applies stun + armor), Bedrock Snare (air+earth, slow->stun escalation). Slow/stun appliers front-loaded so the conditional T2 payoffs (which need existing slow/stun) have something to trigger on. All mana on a 60-mana pool, one free card.

decks.label: Mage loop-5 spellblade splash
decks.notes: L5 hybrid mage leaning on flat-value elemental damage (low INT). Spark opens, free Gust hastes, Mend sustains, Tailwind (air+air, 1m, 4 dmg + haste + refunds 1 mana) is the tempo engine that refuels the mana pool, Steam Surge (fire+water, 6 +4 if burn, heal, +1 stam) cashes the Spark burn and tops resources, Quake adds control, Flame Dart adds a second burn-builder. Mana-dominant with self-refunding cards to stay sustainable on 60 mana.

decks.label: Boss6 Mage Poison Engine
decks.notes: Quadratic-poison ramp tuned for the 460-HP fire dragon. Opens with Slipvenom Tempo (5 poison, doubles to 10 at threshold) and Bog Catalyst (apply 2 + DOUBLE the pool) early so the n(n+1) DoT snowballs immediately; Steaming Plague and Mireglide keep stacks topped while Tidefoot Guard / Brineward provide armor+heal+poison so the mage survives the loop60 race (dragon enrages at 40% HP). Cheap t1-water + Mire Bloom interleave as tempo fillers between the 2-cost both-resource detonate cards so mana never fully starves. Sim: 100% win over 8 reps, ttk 15.0s, 72% HP cushion vs infernal_dragon at runXP 3600 / mult 1.25.

decks.label: Boss6 Mage Burn-Survival
decks.notes: Optimized burn that splashes survival because PURE burn cannot out-race boss6 alone (early all-burn variants died at 0% with ~515/517 damage — a genuine archetype-viability flag). Front-loads cheap appliers (Flame Dart, Pyre) then Magma Welling (14 armor + 8 burn) in slot 3 for the early cushion, Steam Surge heal, and Phoenix Aura (heal 15 + emergency 18 armor under 50% HP) at slot 7. Detonator backbone: Pyre consume+reapply, Cinder Sprint, Cinderlance, Supernova capstone. This armor/heal splash is what flips the matchup. Sim: 100% win over 8 reps, ttk 28.0s, 28% HP cushion (tightest of the lane) vs infernal_dragon.

decks.label: Boss6 Warrior Bleed/Rage
decks.notes: All-stamina warrior so the 50-stamina pool never starves. Opens cheap (Reckless Strike, Bloodprice Strike build rage) then drops Vein Splitter (t3-agility-attack-counter) in slot 3 — its bleed-on-bleed stacking with 0.75 n(n+1) DoT is the run's biggest spike (peakOPS 134) and shreds the dragon almost instantly. Razor Cadence + Necrotic Festering keep stacks climbing; Crimson Spiral is the rage-gated 24-pierce finisher. Sim: 100% win over 8 reps, ttk 7.2s (fastest in lane), 77% HP cushion. The 7s kill is worth noting as borderline-oppressive for the bleed engine vs bosses.

decks.label: Boss6 Warrior Physical Burst
decks.notes: STR-multiplier brawler. Bloodlash Salvo (slot 3, +4 STR this combat) snowballs the universal 1+(STR-1)*0.25 multiplier early, then Triple Slash and Berserker's Ledger deliver high-volume hits while STR is buffed. Mountain's Will (20 STR-scaled + 8 pierce) and Concussive Smash close. Cheap 1-cost openers (Reckless Strike, Quickstrike) keep the deck firing while 2-cost T3s come off cooldown. Sim: 100% win over 8 reps, ttk 9.5s, 69% HP cushion vs infernal_dragon.

decks.label: Boss7 Mage Poison Engine
decks.notes: Same poison-snowball core retuned for the 470-HP defense-affinity golem (loop70). Poison is a DoT that ignores the golem's armor-ratchet shield, so it sidesteps the affinity entirely — Slipvenom Tempo + Bog Catalyst double the pool fast and the n(n+1) realization eats the golem before its 12-shield/7s and enrage matter. Tidefoot Guard / Brineward / Bogplate give armor+heal to absorb the 12-damage hits. Sim: 100% win over 8 reps, ttk 14.0s, 88% HP cushion vs boss_iron_golem at runXP 4400 / mult 1.30.

decks.label: Boss7 Mage Slow/Stun Control
decks.notes: Pure control: freeze the golem's attack cooldown to neutralize its 12-damage hits while slow-detonators chip it down. Cheap t1-earth/Tremor Lock open the slow stacks, Cinder Squall and Gale Echo build to the 5-slow stun threshold, and Tectonic Reckoning (slot 8, 50 STR-pierce + 3 stun, exhaust) is the nuke. Slow is PURE control (no damage) per C5, so the win condition is the air-attack-counter / static-skirmish slow-consume detonators plus Concussive Smash. Slowest clear in lane but safest. Sim: 100% win over 8 reps, ttk 33.6s, 94% HP cushion vs boss_iron_golem.

decks.label: Boss7 Warrior Armor-Pierce Physical
decks.notes: Built around PIERCE to beat the golem's defense-affinity armor ratchet (gains 3 armor/2 = 6 per hit, cap 60). Mountain's Will, Granitewrath, and Cliffwind Maul all deal pierce that ignores the stacking armor, while Triple Slash provides volume and Bloodlash Salvo pumps STR early. The deck keeps an armor side (Earthcleaver / Mountain's Answer gate on own armor) so its own pierce conditionals stay live. Sim: 100% win over 8 reps, ttk 16.8s, 61% HP cushion vs boss_iron_golem — the pierce package is what keeps damage flowing past the ratchet.

decks.label: Boss7 Warrior Armor Finisher
decks.notes: The single sanctioned armor-to-damage line (C3). Cheap guards (Bulwark Vow, Bramble Bulwark, t1-defense) build the armor pool fast, then Aegis of Returning Wrath and Mountain's Answer convert armor into big pierce bursts (Mountain's Answer: 22 pierce when armor >=32). Body Slam Vow / Last Stand Bulwark / Granite Lunge are the per-armor scalers. Against the golem's defensive shield this out-tanks and out-races it. Sim: 100% win over 8 reps, ttk 12.8s, 100% HP cushion (never dipped) vs boss_iron_golem — the tankiest, most reliable endgame warrior shell.

decks.label: Earth/Fire Brace Bulwark (warrior)
decks.notes: Stonepacer (t3-agility-earth-earth) is the cheap 1s vit/haste opener; Slag Maul (t3-attack-earth-fire) the big single-target burn-attack win-con; Magmavow (t3-counter-earth-fire) the Brace armor->16 Pierce+burn payoff. Front-loaded t1/t2 1s armor so Brace triggers reliably. All stamina (warrior). SIM (5 reps) vs doom_knight: winRate 1.0, 100% HP cushion, TTK 34.9s, 393 dmg — wins but armor wall trivializes the boss (no damage taken).

decks.label: Earth/Fire Bulwark vs loop15 normals
decks.notes: Same Stonepacer/Magmavow/Slag Maul bulwark core at a second difficulty (1.15 normal mult, runXP 900) for a >1-difficulty datapoint. Leaner 10-card list to keep stamina economy honest. SIM (5 reps) vs mutated_salamander/werewolf/depths_horror: all winRate 1.0, 100% HP, TTK 12.5-17.1s — strong unscathed wall vs normals.

decks.label: Reactive Bleed/Rage Wall (warrior)
decks.notes: Bramble Step (t3-agility-counter-defense) armor+Brace-bleed wall; Bloodtide Mend (t2-counter-water) self-bleed heal + Vengeance spi sustain; Vengeful Pyre (t3-counter-counter-fire) the exhaust rage-doubler. Counter/Vengeance fed by doom_knight hits. All stamina. SIM (5 reps) vs doom_knight: winRate 1.0, 37.7% HP cushion, TTK 31.5s, 422 dmg — exactly the healthy non-trivial first-boss profile.

decks.label: Reactive Bleed Wall vs loop15 normals
decks.notes: Bramble Step + Bloodtide Mend reactive core at 1.15 mult / runXP 900 for a 2nd difficulty datapoint; dropped the exhaust Vengeful Pyre to isolate the bleed/heal cards. SIM (5 reps) vs mutated_salamander/werewolf/depths_horror: all winRate 1.0, 74.7-98.2% HP, TTK 9.5-14.0s — reactive sustain holds comfortably.

decks.label: Elemental Armor-Control Hybrid (mage)
decks.notes: Centers the 'both'-resource T3 hybrids: Pyric Bulwark (t3-defense-defense-fire armor->burn), Quickstone (t3-agility-defense-earth armor+conditional haste), Searing Razor (t3-agility-counter-fire burn+dex), Drowner's Dart (t3-agility-attack-water poison-pierce win-con), Tidefoot Bloom (t3-counter-water-water heal->poison engine, paired with t1-water Mend). Each only 1s+1m so the 60-mana pool + light stamina sustains them; cheap t1-fire/t1-water/t2-agility-fire openers front-loaded. SIM (5 reps) vs doom_knight: winRate 1.0 but only 14% HP cushion, TTK 39s, 386 dmg — a real nail-biter, the slow DoT/control plan barely closes the boss.

decks.label: Elemental Hybrid vs boss2 (iron_golem)
decks.notes: Same hybrid control core vs iron_golem (1.05 boss mult, runXP 1150, defense-affinity armor ratchet) to stress the poison/burn DoT lines (Drowner's Dart, Searing Razor, Tidefoot Bloom) and the armor/haste survival cards (Pyric Bulwark, Quickstone) against a tanky boss at a 2nd difficulty. SIM (5 reps): winRate 1.0, 72.9% HP cushion, TTK 40s, 411 dmg — closes the 409-HP/def-4 golem comfortably; armor+haste survival carries the slow DoT clock.

decks.label: Stamina armor bulwark (fire/earth/counter walls)
decks.notes: Covers t2-defense-fire, t3-counter-defense-fire (Ashen Bulwark), t3-air-defense-defense (Stormgate), t3-counter-counter-earth (Stonewrath). Pure-stamina armor wall; Aegis + Mountain's Answer are the armor->damage finisher line. SIM vs doom_knight (loop10, 1.0): winRate 1.0/5, 0 deaths, TTK 30.6s, hero ends 100% HP, 429 dmg, 18 cards. Walls survive trivially; the finisher closes. Slow TTK but healthy first-boss profile.

decks.label: Stamina armor bulwark vs late band (1.15x normals + iron_golem)
decks.notes: Same armor deck one band harder (expander 'late': runXP720, 1.15x normals + iron_golem 1.05). SIM 5 reps each: winRate 1.0 vs depths_horror/fire_elemental/werewolf/mutated_salamander AND iron_golem boss; hero stays 100% HP on every fight; iron_golem TTK 29.2s / 454 dmg / 17 cards. The assigned armor T3s (defense-fire vit-stack, Stormgate, Stonewrath low-HP rage, Ashen Bulwark) are durable but low-OPS; closing relies on the finisher line.

decks.label: Agility-air slow tempo vs late band (1.15x + iron_golem)
decks.notes: Covers t3-agility-air-attack (Skywire), t3-agility-air-fire (Galecinder), t3-agility-air-counter (Static Skirmish), t3-air-air-counter (Stormrage, rage-gated slow). Both-cost cards but mostly stamina -> warrior. SIM 5 reps: winRate 1.0 on all 4 normals + iron_golem. Real HP cost as difficulty climbs: depths_horror 92% HP, werewolf 81%, mutated_salamander 73%, iron_golem 45% HP / TTK 30.3s / 23 cards. This is the most racing deck; slow buys time while Skywire/Galecinder chip. Stormrage's gate on having rage is the weak link in a low-rage shell.

decks.label: Agility-air slow tempo vs doom_knight
decks.notes: Same tempo deck vs the easy first boss. SIM vs doom_knight (loop10, 1.0): winRate 1.0/5, 0 deaths, TTK 26.1s, hero ends 39.7% HP, 396 dmg, 19 cards. Beatable but with meaningful HP cost - matches the 'should be winnable, not trivial' first-boss target. Slow control + agility chip is the win-con; the assigned slow cards earn their slot.

decks.label: Mage air tempo / control (mana)
decks.notes: Covers t3-air-air-air (Tempest Cadence, AoE win-con), t3-air-counter-water (Tempestbleed, spi+slow), t3-air-air-defense (Galeward, armor+haste). Air slow/stun control mage. SIM vs doom_knight (loop10, 1.0): winRate 1.0/5, 0 deaths, hero ends 91.7% HP, but TTK 37.0s with 47 cards played / 4 reshuffles. Low-INT flat air damage + haste volume grinds slowly - survivable, very slow to close. Flag if the audit wants faster mage closers.

decks.label: Mage air tempo / control vs late band (1.15x + iron_golem)
decks.notes: Same air mage deck one band harder. SIM 5 reps: winRate 1.0 on all 4 normals + iron_golem; hero stays 90-96% HP throughout (very tanky via Galeward armor + slow control). But cardsPlayed is very high (16-27 normals, 48 vs iron_golem, 4 reshuffles) and iron_golem TTK 37.0s - Tempest Cadence at low INT is the only real damage and it closes slowly. Strong survival, weak kill-speed: candidate off_curve_weak for the air-damage line at low INT.

decks.label: Warrior fire-bulwark (Forge Strike / Tombrage)
decks.notes: Centers t3-attack-defense-fire (Forge Strike: 12 dmg + 8 armor + 2 burn) and t3-counter-earth-earth (Tombrage: 12 armor + low-HP rage); t2-air-defense (Cyclone Ward) is the cheap armor/haste filler. SIM (5 reps, expander stage boss1 = doom_knight L10): vs doom_knight wr=1.00, ttk~26.5s, HP cushion 82%, 0 deaths. Healthy first-boss profile; assigned cards all reliably playable on the 50 stamina pool.

decks.label: Warrior burn-tempo + burn->armor convert
decks.notes: Centers t3-agility-agility-fire (Twinflame Flicker: cheap 1s 4-burn applier) feeding t3-air-defense-fire (Ember Aegis Gust: consume burn -> armor). Burn engine = Twinflame + Kindle Strike + Cinder Thrust; converter banks the pool into armor. SIM (5 reps, expander stage late = scaled normals + iron_golem): clears full battery wr=1.00 (depths_horror/fire_elemental/werewolf/mut_salamander HP cushion 86-96%) AND iron_golem wr=1.00 (ttk~38s, HP 76%). t2-air-defense pulls double duty as armor filler.

decks.label: Mage earth-fire armor/vengeance
decks.notes: Centers t3-earth-earth-fire (Magma Vow: 2m, 14 armor + Vengeance 14 Pierce) and t3-agility-earth-fire (Cinderquake: 1m, 10 dmg, armor>=10 pierce+burn rider). Magma Vein/Magma Vow build the armor that arms Cinderquake. SIM (5 reps, expander stage late = iron_golem boss2): clears the normal battery wr=1.00 (HP 58-91%), but vs iron_golem wr=0.00, 5 deaths, 31 cards played -> the deck STALLS. FINDING (off_curve_weak / progression, med): the mana-throttled earth-fire armor core has no answer to the iron_golem defense-affinity armor ratchet; Magma Vow's win condition is Vengeance-ONLY 14 Pierce on a 2.4s CD, too conditional/slow to close a high-armor boss. C1-compliant rescue would be cutting Cinderquake/Magma Vow CD or giving Magma Vow a non-Vengeance baseline hit, never a cost bump.

decks.label: Reactive armor-control bulwark (both-resource)
decks.notes: Centers three 1s+1m 'both'-resource armor/control T3s: t3-agility-counter-earth (Quickearth Rite, VIT scaling), t3-counter-defense-water (Crimson Regen Mantle, 20% DR + low-HP regen), t3-agility-defense-fire (Ember Vault, armor + Brace burn). Warrior funds the stamina halves from 50 pool; the 1-mana halves are covered by +1 mana/4.5s regen spread across the fight. SIM (5 reps, expander stage boss1 = doom_knight): wr=1.00, ttk~47.8s, HP cushion 97%, 0 deaths -- the reactive armor stack trivializes incoming damage (race-losing but rock-solid survival, per C3). 31 cards over the fight confirms the 'both' cards stay affordable despite the slow mana drip.

decks.label: Poison-rage bulwark (Bogwrath)
decks.notes: Centers t3-counter-earth-water (Bogwrath: 10 armor, Brace -> 4 poison + 3 rage) as a defensive poison seed, fed by a poison engine (Mire Bloom, Bog Catalyst doubler, Steaming Plague, Alchemic Drain) plus t3-agility-agility-fire (Twinflame) and t1-fire for an off-axis burn. SIM (5 reps, expander stage late): clears full normal battery wr=1.00 (HP 85-94%) and iron_golem wr=1.00 (ttk~22s, HP 69%) -- poison DoT bypasses the golem's armor ratchet, making this a notably stronger boss profile than the pure earth-fire armor deck (cover-2-3).

decks.label: Agility armor-tempo bulwark (warrior)
decks.notes: Centers three cheap (1s) low-cd agility armor+haste cards: Veil of Steps (14 armor), Footwork Stone (12 armor), Galeguard (10 armor). Haste stacking accelerates cheap attack openers into a Berserker's Ledger finisher. SIM (5 reps) vs doom_knight: win 1.00, ttk 25.9s, 96% HP, 0 deaths/timeouts, 27 cards. Armor-tempo buys time cleanly vs the 9-dmg boss.

decks.label: Agility armor-tempo bulwark (loop15)
decks.notes: Same agility armor-tempo core at a harder normal band (runXP 900, late battery, mult ~1.1) so Veil of Steps / Footwork Stone / Galeguard are seen at >1 difficulty. SIM vs depths_horror/fire_elemental/werewolf: win 1.00, ttk 7.9-9.3s, 94-97% HP, 0 deaths/timeouts. Cheap armor+haste tempo still trivializes scaled normals.

decks.label: Poison-bruiser armor hybrid (warrior)
decks.notes: Hybrid bruiser around Soaking Blade (12+2 poison+7), Mire Cleave (10+8 armor+2 poison, only both-resource card), Magmaplate (16 armor, Brace: 14+2 burn). Cheap physical openers build pressure while armor/poison sustains. SIM vs doom_knight: win 1.00, ttk 24.7s, 79% HP, 0 deaths/timeouts, 14 cards. Healthy boss profile with HP cost.

decks.label: Poison-bruiser armor hybrid (boss2)
